title: Excuse of Conditions — Waiver
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A condition may be excused by waiver when a party voluntarily relinquishes the right to enforce the condition.

title: Excuse to Statutory Violation
subject: Torts
ruleText: A statutory violation may be excused when compliance would be more dangerous, impossible, or beyond the actor's knowledge despite reasonable care, or where another recognized excuse applies.

title: Execution of Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Execution of a deed requires the grantor’s signature and compliance with any required formalities.

title: Executive Agreements
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Executive agreements made by the President usually do not require Senate approval and generally prevail over conflicting state law, but they do not prevail over existing federal statutes or the Constitution.

title: Executive Orders
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The President may issue executive orders pursuant to constitutional or statutory authority.

title: Executive Power — General
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The President has the power to execute the laws and conduct foreign affairs.

title: Executive Privilege
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The President has a qualified privilege to withhold confidential presidential communications, especially in matters of national security or foreign affairs, but the privilege may yield to demonstrated need in criminal proceedings.

title: Executory Interest
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An executory interest is a future interest in a transferee that divests or cuts short another interest or begins after a gap in possession.

title: Exigent Circumstances
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Warrantless action is allowed when urgent needs such as hot pursuit, risk to safety, or imminent destruction of evidence make waiting unreasonable.

title: Exigent Circumstances
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may conduct a warrantless search when exigent circumstances make it impracticable to obtain a warrant.

title: Exigent Circumstances — Examples
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Exigent circumstances include hot pursuit, risk of destruction of evidence, and emergency aid situations.

title: Exoneration of Liens
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Under modern law, a specific devisee generally takes devised property subject to any mortgage or lien existing at death unless the will directs exoneration.

title: Expectation Damages
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Expectation damages put the injured party in the position the party would have occupied had the contract been fully performed.
buzzwords: position had the contract been fully performed

title: Expectation Damages
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Expectation damages compensate the non-breaching party for the benefit of the bargain.

title: Expectation of Privacy in Home
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The home receives the highest Fourth Amendment protection, and warrantless entry is presumptively unreasonable absent a recognized exception.

title: Expert Disclosures
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Parties generally must disclose expert witnesses and provide the required expert information or reports within the time set by rule or court order.

title: Expert Discovery
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Parties must disclose expert witnesses and provide written reports for retained experts.

title: Expert Opinion — Criminal Mental State Limitation
subject: Evidence
ruleText: An expert may not state an opinion about whether a criminal defendant had the required mental state constituting an element of the crime.

title: Expert Qualification
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness who is qualified as an expert by knowledge, skill, experience, training, or education may testify in the form of an opinion if the expert's specialized knowledge will help the trier of fact, the testimony is based on sufficient facts or data, the testimony is the product of reliable principles and methods, and the expert has reliably applied the principles and methods to the facts of the case.

title: Expert Qualification
subject: Evidence
ruleText: An expert may be qualified by knowledge, skill, experience, training, or education.

title: Expert Testimony — General Rule
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Expert testimony is admissible if the expert is qualified and the testimony will help the trier of fact understand the evidence or determine a fact in issue.

title: Expert Testimony — Reliability
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Expert testimony must be based on sufficient facts or data and reliable principles and methods reliably applied to the facts of the case.

title: Express Actual Authority
subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
ruleText: An agent has express actual authority when the principal expressly communicates to the agent authority to act on the principal's behalf.

title: Express Conditions
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An express condition is a condition that is explicitly stated in the contract language.

title: Express Easement
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An express easement is created by a writing satisfying the Statute of Frauds or by deed reservation or grant.

title: Express Warranty
subject: Torts
ruleText: An express warranty is created by an affirmation of fact, promise, description, or sample that becomes part of the basis of the bargain.

title: Express Warranty
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An express warranty is created by an affirmation of fact, promise, description, or sample that becomes part of the basis of the bargain.

title: Extortion
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Extortion is obtaining property by means of threats of future harm, unlike robbery which requires immediate force or intimidation.

title: Extortion
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Extortion is obtaining property through threats of future harm or exposure.

title: Facial Discrimination
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A law that discriminates on its face is subject to the level of scrutiny appropriate for the classification used.

title: Factors for Abnormally Dangerous Activities
subject: Torts
ruleText: An activity is abnormally dangerous based on factors including high risk of harm, likelihood of great harm, inability to eliminate risk with reasonable care, uncommon usage, and inappropriateness of location.

title: Failed Residuary Gifts
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Under the modern rule, if one share of a residuary gift fails, the other residuary beneficiaries typically take the failed share in proportion to their interests rather than the failed portion passing by intestacy.

title: Failure to Join a Required Party
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A Rule 12(b)(7) motion challenges the failure to join a party required under the joinder rules.

title: Failure to Provide Adequate Assurances
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Failure to provide adequate assurances within a reasonable time constitutes a repudiation of the contract.

title: Failure to State a Claim
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A Rule 12(b)(6) motion should be granted when the complaint fails to allege sufficient facts to state a plausible claim for relief.

title: Failure to Warn
subject: Torts
ruleText: A product is defective for failure to warn when foreseeable risks of harm could have been reduced or avoided by reasonable instructions or warnings and the omission renders the product not reasonably safe.

title: Failure to Warn — Defect
subject: Torts
ruleText: A product is defective due to failure to warn when foreseeable risks of harm could have been reduced or avoided by adequate warnings.

title: Failure to Warn — Obvious Dangers
subject: Torts
ruleText: There is no duty to warn of risks that are open and obvious to a reasonable user.

title: Fair Housing
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Federal fair housing law generally prohibits discrimination in the sale or rental of housing on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, familial status, national origin, and disability, subject to limited exceptions.

title: Fairness Factors
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may consider the burden on the defendant, the forum state's interest, the plaintiff's interest in convenient relief, the interstate judicial system's interest in efficiency, and shared policy interests of the states.

title: Fairness Factors
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Courts consider the burden on the defendant, the forum state’s interest, the plaintiff’s interest, and judicial efficiency.

title: False Imprisonment
subject: Torts
ruleText: False imprisonment occurs when a defendant intentionally confines another person without lawful privilege and without consent.

title: False Imprisonment
subject: Torts
ruleText: False imprisonment requires an intentional act that confines the plaintiff to a bounded area without lawful privilege and against the plaintiff's will, and the plaintiff is aware of the confinement or harmed by it.

title: False Imprisonment
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: False imprisonment is the unlawful confinement of a person without consent or legal authority.

title: False Light
subject: Torts
ruleText: False light occurs when a defendant publicly attributes false information to the plaintiff that would be highly offensive to a reasonable person.

title: False Light
subject: Torts
ruleText: False light occurs when the defendant publicizes matter placing the plaintiff in a false light that would be highly offensive to a reasonable person, subject to constitutional limits in matters of public concern.

title: False Pretenses
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: False pretenses occurs when title to property is obtained through a false representation of a material fact with intent to defraud.

title: False Pretenses
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: False pretenses is obtaining title to another's personal property by intentional false representation of a material past or present fact with intent to defraud.

title: Family Records
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Statements of fact about personal or family history contained in a family record, such as a Bible, genealogy, engraving on a ring, inscription on a portrait, or engraving on an urn or burial marker, are admissible.

title: Farm Products
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Farm products are goods, other than standing timber, with respect to which the debtor is engaged in a farming operation and that are crops, livestock, supplies used or produced in farming, or products of crops or livestock in their unmanufactured states.

title: Fault-Based Divorce
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Traditional fault-based grounds include adultery, cruelty, desertion, habitual drunkenness, imprisonment, and similar misconduct, depending on the statute.

title: Federal Powers — Enumerated Powers
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may act only pursuant to powers enumerated in the Constitution.

title: Federal Procedural Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts apply valid Federal Rules of Civil Procedure when they directly conflict with state law.

title: Federal Question Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts have jurisdiction over civil actions arising under the Constitution, laws, or treaties of the United States.

title: Federal Question Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal question jurisdiction exists when a claim arises under the Constitution, laws, or treaties of the United States.

title: Federal Question Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal question jurisdiction exists when the plaintiff's well pleaded complaint shows that the claim arises under federal law.

title: Fee Simple Absolute
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A fee simple absolute is the largest estate in land, of potentially infinite duration, and is freely alienable, devisable, and descendible.

title: Fee Simple Determinable
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A fee simple determinable ends automatically upon the occurrence of a stated event and is typically created by durational language such as 'so long as,' 'while,' or 'until.'

title: Fee Simple Subject to Condition Subsequent
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A fee simple subject to condition subsequent does not end automatically, but may be cut short at the grantor's election upon breach of a stated condition.

title: Fee Simple Subject to Executory Limitation
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A fee simple subject to executory limitation is a defeasible fee that, upon the stated event, automatically shifts to a third party rather than reverting to the grantor.

title: Fee Tail
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A fee tail is an estate that descends automatically to the grantee's lineal descendants and, in most jurisdictions, is either abolished or converted into a fee simple absolute.

title: Felony Murder
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Felony murder is a killing caused during the commission or attempted commission of a qualifying felony, without need to prove malice separately.

title: Felony Murder — Agency Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the agency rule, felony murder liability applies only when the killing is committed by the defendant or a co-felon.

title: Felony Murder — Inherently Dangerous Felony
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Felony murder applies only when the underlying felony is inherently dangerous to human life.

title: Felony Murder — Merger Doctrine
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The felony murder doctrine does not apply when the underlying felony merges with the homicide.

title: Felony Murder — Proximate Cause Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the proximate cause rule, felony murder liability applies if the death is a foreseeable result of the felony.

title: Fifth Amendment — Self-Incrimination
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Fifth Amendment protects individuals from being compelled to incriminate themselves.

title: Fighting Words
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Fighting words are personally abusive epithets inherently likely to provoke an immediate violent reaction, and they may be regulated in narrow circumstances.

title: Final Judgment Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Only final judgments resolving all claims and parties are appealable as of right.

title: Final Judgment Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: As a general rule, only final judgments that resolve all claims as to all parties are immediately appealable.

title: Financing Statement Requirements
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A financing statement is sufficient if it provides the debtor's name, the name of the secured party or its representative, and indicates the collateral covered.

title: Firm Offer Under UCC
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A merchant's signed written offer assuring that it will be held open is irrevocable for the time stated, or for a reasonable time if none is stated, not exceeding three months.

title: Firm Offers (UCC)
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, a merchant’s signed written offer to buy or sell goods is irrevocable for the time stated or a reasonable time not exceeding three months.

title: Fixture Filing
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A fixture filing is a financing statement covering goods that are or are to become fixtures and that is filed in the office where a mortgage on the related real property would be recorded and indicates that it covers fixtures, indicates the real property, and, if the debtor does not have an interest of record in the real property, provides the name of the record owner.

title: Foreclosure
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Foreclosure is the process by which the mortgagee cuts off the mortgagor's equity of redemption and sells the property to satisfy the debt.

title: Foreclosure — General Rule
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Foreclosure terminates the mortgagor’s equity of redemption and junior interests.

title: Foreseeability Test
subject: Torts
ruleText: Under the foreseeability test, proximate cause exists if the type of harm suffered was foreseeable.

title: Foreseeable Plaintiffs
subject: Torts
ruleText: A duty is ordinarily owed only to those plaintiffs who are within the zone of foreseeable risk created by the defendant's conduct.

title: Forfeiture by Wrongdoing
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A party forfeits the right to object to hearsay when the party intentionally causes the declarant’s unavailability to prevent testimony.

title: Forfeiture by Wrongdoing
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement offered against a party that wrongfully caused, or acquiesced in wrongfully causing, the declarant's unavailability as a witness, and did so intending that result, is admissible under the forfeiture-by-wrongdoing exception.

title: Forfeiture by Wrongdoing — Confrontation Clause
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A defendant forfeits Confrontation Clause rights by intentionally causing a witness’s unavailability to prevent testimony.

title: Forgery
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Forgery is the making or altering of a writing with intent to defraud so that it purports to be the act of another.

title: Formal Requirements — Attested Will
subject: Trusts
ruleText: An attested will must be in writing, signed by the testator, and witnessed by at least two witnesses.

title: Formal Requirements for an Attested Will
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: An attested will generally must be in writing, signed by the testator or by another at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence, and signed by the required number of witnesses within the time and manner required by statute.

title: Formal Validity of a Will
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: A will is often treated as formally valid if it complies with the law of the place where executed, the law of the testator's domicile at execution or death, or the law of the situs for real property, depending on the governing statute.

title: Former Testimony
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Former testimony given as a witness at a trial, hearing, or lawful deposition is admissible if the declarant is unavailable and the party against whom it is now offered, or in a civil case the party's predecessor in interest, had an opportunity and similar motive to develop the testimony by direct, cross-, or redirect examination.

title: Former Testimony
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Former testimony is admissible if the declarant is unavailable and the testimony was given under oath with an opportunity for cross-examination by a party with similar motive.

title: Forum Defendant Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A diversity case generally may not be removed if any properly joined and served defendant is a citizen of the state in which the action was filed.

title: Forum Non Conveniens
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may dismiss under forum non conveniens when another available and adequate forum is substantially more convenient and transfer is not available.

title: Forum Non Conveniens — Factors
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Private factors include access to evidence and witnesses, while public factors include court congestion and local interest.

title: Forum Non Conveniens — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may dismiss a case under forum non conveniens when there is an alternative forum that is substantially more appropriate.

title: Forum Non Conveniens — Requirements
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Forum non conveniens requires an available and adequate alternative forum and a balance of private and public factors favoring dismissal.

title: Forum Selection Clauses
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Valid forum selection clauses are generally enforced unless unreasonable or unjust.

title: Fourth Amendment — Scope
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Fourth Amendment protects against unreasonable searches and seizures by the government.

title: Fraud in the Execution
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Fraud in the execution occurs when the testator is deceived about the nature of the document being signed, such that the testator does not realize it is a will or does not understand its contents.

title: Fraud in the Inducement
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Fraud in the inducement occurs when the testator knowingly executes a will but is induced by a knowingly false representation to make a disposition the testator would not otherwise have made.

title: Fraud Pleading Standard
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Fraud must be pleaded with particularity, although conditions of mind such as malice, intent, knowledge, and other mental states may be alleged generally.

title: Fraudulent Misrepresentation
subject: Torts
ruleText: Fraudulent misrepresentation requires a material misrepresentation of present or past fact made with scienter, intent to induce reliance, actual justifiable reliance, and damages.

title: Free Exercise — Neutral Laws of General Applicability
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Neutral laws of general applicability that incidentally burden religion are constitutional.

title: Free Exercise — Targeting Religion
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Laws that target religious conduct are subject to strict scrutiny.

title: Free Exercise and Neutral Laws
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A neutral law of general applicability that incidentally burdens religion usually does not violate the Free Exercise Clause and is reviewed under rational basis, not strict scrutiny.

title: Free Exercise Clause — General Rule
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The Free Exercise Clause prohibits the government from substantially burdening religious exercise without sufficient justification.

title: Freedom of Speech — General Rule
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The First Amendment prohibits the government from abridging freedom of speech.

title: Frolic and Detour
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A detour is a minor deviation from assigned work that remains within the scope of employment, while a frolic is a substantial departure for personal purposes that takes the employee outside the scope of employment until the employee returns to the employer's business.

title: From the Person or Presence
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Property is taken from the person or presence if it is taken from the victim's body or area of immediate control.

title: Fruit of an Involuntary Confession
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence derived from an involuntary confession is inadmissible as fruit of the poisonous tree.

title: Fruit of the Poisonous Tree
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Derivative evidence obtained as a result of unconstitutional government conduct is generally excluded unless sufficiently attenuated, independently sourced, or inevitably discoverable.

title: Fruit of the Poisonous Tree
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The fruit of the poisonous tree doctrine excludes evidence derived from an initial illegal search or seizure.

title: Frustration of Purpose
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Frustration of purpose discharges contractual duties when an unforeseen event substantially frustrates the contract’s principal purpose.

title: Frustration of Purpose
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Performance is excused by frustration of purpose when an unforeseen event substantially destroys the principal purpose of the contract and both parties understood that purpose.

title: Full Faith and Credit
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts must give state court judgments the same preclusive effect they would receive in the rendering state.

title: Full Faith and Credit
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: States generally must recognize valid judgments from sister states if the rendering court had jurisdiction and the judgment is final and on the merits.

title: Full Faith and Credit for Divorce Decrees
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A divorce decree entered by a state with proper jurisdiction based on domicile is generally entitled to full faith and credit in other states, although collateral attack may be allowed on jurisdictional grounds in some circumstances.

title: Full Faith and Credit to Judgments
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Under the Full Faith and Credit Clause, a valid final judgment rendered by a court with proper jurisdiction generally must be recognized and enforced by sister states.

title: Fundamental Rights
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Fundamental rights include marriage, privacy, contraception, family relationships, child rearing, and interstate travel.

title: Fundamental Rights and Strict Scrutiny
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A law that substantially burdens a fundamental right is subject to strict scrutiny and must be narrowly tailored to serve a compelling governmental interest.

title: Future Advance Mortgage
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A mortgage securing future advances may have priority from the time of initial recording, though optional advances made with notice of an intervening lien may lose priority in some jurisdictions.

title: Future Advance Mortgage
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A mortgage securing future advances has priority from the date of recording.

title: Future Advances
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A perfected security interest secures future advances if the security agreement so provides, and the priority of those future advances generally relates back to the original filing or perfection against competing secured parties and lien creditors, subject to special rules in some contexts.

title: Future Covenants
subject: Real Property
ruleText: The future covenants are warranty, quiet enjoyment, and further assurances, and they are breached only when the grantee is later disturbed in possession or title.

title: Future Interest — Executory Interest
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An executory interest is a future interest held by a third party that divests a prior estate upon the occurrence of a condition.

title: Future Interest — Possibility of Reverter
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A possibility of reverter is a future interest retained by the grantor following a fee simple determinable.

title: Future Interest — Remainder
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A remainder is a future interest that becomes possessory upon the natural termination of a prior estate.

title: Future Interest — Right of Entry
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A right of entry is a future interest retained by the grantor following a fee simple subject to condition subsequent.

title: General and Special Powers
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A general power of appointment may be exercised in favor of the donee, the donee's estate, the donee's creditors, or the creditors of the donee's estate, while a special power may be exercised only in favor of a limited class excluding those persons.

title: General Authentication Requirement
subject: Evidence
ruleText: To authenticate or identify an item of evidence, the proponent must produce evidence sufficient to support a finding that the item is what the proponent claims it is.

title: General Competency
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Every person is competent to be a witness unless the rules provide otherwise. Competency usually requires personal knowledge, an oath or affirmation, and the ability to perceive, remember, and communicate.

title: General Duty of Care
subject: Torts
ruleText: A defendant generally owes a duty to exercise the care that a reasonably prudent person would use under like circumstances to avoid foreseeable risks of harm to foreseeable plaintiffs.

title: General Fixture Priority Rule
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A perfected security interest in fixtures generally is subordinate to a conflicting interest of an encumbrancer or owner of the related real property unless a special fixture-priority rule applies, such as purchase-money fixture priority or prior perfection before affixation.
buzzwords: encumbrancer or owner of real property

title: General Intangibles
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: General intangibles are personal property, including things in action, other than accounts, chattel paper, deposit accounts, documents, goods, instruments, investment property, letter-of-credit rights, money, and oil, gas, or other minerals before extraction.

title: General Intent Crimes
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A general intent crime requires only awareness of acting in the proscribed manner, without any further purpose.

title: General Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: General jurisdiction exists when the defendant is essentially at home in the forum, so the court may hear any claim against that defendant.

title: General Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: General jurisdiction exists when the defendant’s contacts with the forum are so continuous and systematic as to render the defendant essentially at home in the forum.

title: General Jurisdiction — Corporations
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A corporation is subject to general jurisdiction in its state of incorporation and principal place of business.

title: General Methods of Perfection
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A security interest is generally perfected by filing a financing statement, taking possession of the collateral, obtaining control of the collateral, or, in some cases, automatically upon attachment.

title: General Partner Liability in LP
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A general partner in a limited partnership generally has management rights and is personally liable for the obligations of the limited partnership, much like a partner in a general partnership, unless the general partner is itself an entity providing liability protection.

title: General Partnership Formation
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A general partnership is formed when two or more persons associate to carry on as co-owners a business for profit, whether or not they intend to form a partnership.

title: General Pleading Standard
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A complaint must contain a short and plain statement showing that the pleader is entitled to relief and enough factual matter to state a plausible claim for relief.

title: General Priority Rule
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: As between conflicting perfected security interests in the same collateral, priority generally dates from the earlier of filing or perfection, provided there is no period thereafter when there is neither filing nor perfection.

title: General Warranty Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A general warranty deed provides warranties against defects in title arising before and during the grantor’s ownership.

title: General Warranty Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A general warranty deed includes the present covenants of seisin, right to convey, and against encumbrances, and the future covenants of warranty, quiet enjoyment, and further assurances.

title: Good Faith — Limitations
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The good faith exception does not apply when the magistrate is biased, the warrant is facially deficient, or officers misled the magistrate.

title: Good Faith — Limits
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The good faith exception does not apply when police rely on a warrant that is facially deficient, issued by a biased magistrate, or based on false information.

title: Good Faith Exception
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The exclusionary rule does not apply when police act in objectively reasonable reliance on a warrant later found invalid.

title: Good Faith. Exception
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence obtained under a defective warrant is admissible if police acted in objectively reasonable reliance on the warrant.

title: Good-Faith Exception
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence need not be excluded when police reasonably rely in good faith on a warrant, statute, or binding precedent later found defective, unless reliance was objectively unreasonable.

title: Goods
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Goods are all things that are movable when a security interest attaches, including fixtures, standing timber to be cut, and unborn young of animals, but not money, documents, instruments, accounts, chattel paper, or general intangibles.

title: Government Action Requirement
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Fourth Amendment applies only to government action and not to searches conducted by private individuals acting independently.

title: Government Employment and Religion
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The government may not condition public employment on religious belief or practice.

title: Guardianship of Children
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A court may appoint a guardian for a child when the child's parents are unavailable, unfit, deceased, or when guardianship is otherwise necessary under statute and consistent with the child's best interests.

title: Guideline Child Support
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Child support is generally calculated under statutory guidelines that consider parental income, number of children, childcare costs, health insurance, extraordinary expenses, and time spent with each parent, subject to deviations when justified.

title: Guilty Pleas
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A guilty plea must be voluntary, knowing, and intelligent, with understanding of the charge, major consequences, and rights waived.

title: Habit and Routine Practice
subject: Evidence
subtopic: Relevance and Reasons for Excluding Relevant Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of a person's habit or an organization's routine practice may be admitted to prove that on a particular occasion the person or organization acted in accordance with the habit or routine practice.

title: Habit Evidence
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of a person’s habit or an organization’s routine practice is admissible to prove conduct in conformity with the habit or routine.

title: Half-Blood Relatives
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: In most jurisdictions, half-blood relatives are treated the same as whole-blood relatives for intestate succession, unless a statute provides otherwise.

title: Hanna Test
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Under Hanna, a valid Federal Rule applies if it is arguably procedural and within the Rules Enabling Act.

title: Harmless Error
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A conviction will not be reversed if the admission of illegally obtained evidence was harmless beyond a reasonable doubt.

title: Harmless Error — Trial Rights
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A constitutional trial error does not require reversal if it was harmless beyond a reasonable doubt.

title: Harmless Error Rule
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Under the harmless error doctrine, a document not executed in strict compliance with will formalities may still be treated as a will if there is clear and convincing evidence that the decedent intended the document to constitute the decedent's will or amendment.

title: Hate Speech
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Hate speech is generally protected unless it falls into an unprotected category such as incitement or true threats.

title: Hearsay — Definition
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Hearsay is an out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted.

title: Hearsay Within Hearsay
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Hearsay within hearsay is admissible only if each layer of the combined statements conforms with a hearsay exception or exclusion.

title: Heightened Pleading — Rule 9(b)
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Claims of fraud or mistake must be pleaded with particularity regarding the circumstances constituting the fraud or mistake.

title: Hierarchy of Interpretation Evidence
subject: Contracts
ruleText: In interpreting contracts, courts consider express terms first, then course of performance, course of dealing, and usage of trade.

title: Holographic Will
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A holographic will is generally valid if the material portions are in the testator's handwriting and the document is signed by the testator, if recognized by the jurisdiction.

title: Holographic Will
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A holographic will is valid if it is entirely in the testator’s handwriting and signed.

title: Home State Jurisdiction under UCCJEA
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Under the UCCJEA, the child's home state, usually the state where the child lived with a parent for at least six consecutive months immediately before commencement of the custody proceeding, has primary jurisdiction to make an initial custody determination.

title: Homestead, Exempt Property, and Family Allowance
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Many statutes provide a surviving spouse and sometimes minor children with homestead allowance, exempt property, and a temporary family allowance payable off the top of the estate, often in addition to intestate or elective-share rights.

title: Homicide — Definition
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Homicide is the unlawful killing of another human being.

title: Homicide Case Peacefulness Exception
subject: Evidence
subtopic: Relevance and Reasons for Excluding Relevant Evidence
ruleText: In a homicide case, the prosecutor may offer evidence of the alleged victim's trait of peacefulness to rebut evidence that the victim was the first aggressor.

title: Honorary Trust
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: An honorary trust is a noncharitable trust without an enforceable human beneficiary, often for a pet or grave maintenance, and is valid only to the extent permitted by statute or modern doctrine.

title: Horizontal Privity
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Horizontal privity requires the covenant to be made in connection with a transfer of land.

title: Hostile Possession
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Hostile possession means possession without the true owner's permission and under a claim inconsistent with the owner's rights.

title: Hostile Witness
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The court may permit a party to use leading questions with a hostile witness, an adverse party, or a witness identified with an adverse party.

title: Identifiable Proceeds
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A security interest continues in identifiable proceeds, and proceeds are identifiable if they can be traced back to the original collateral, including by equitable tracing methods such as the lowest intermediate balance rule for commingled cash proceeds.

title: Identification of Goods
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Goods are identified to the contract when they are designated as the goods to which the contract refers.

title: Illegality
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract is unenforceable if its subject matter or performance is illegal.

title: Illegality
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract with illegal subject matter or purpose is generally unenforceable.

title: Illusory Promise
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An illusory promise is not consideration because it does not actually bind the promisor to do anything.

title: Illusory Promises
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An illusory promise is not consideration because it does not bind the promisor to any performance.

title: Immovables and Marital Property
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Marital property rights in real property are generally determined by the law of the situs of the land.

title: Impartial Jury
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to an impartial jury.

title: Impeachment
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Federal officials may be impeached by the House of Representatives and convicted by the Senate for treason, bribery, or other high crimes and misdemeanors.

title: Impeachment
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The House has the sole power to impeach by majority vote, and the Senate tries impeachments and convicts by two-thirds vote; removal follows conviction.

title: Impeachment — Bias or Interest
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by showing bias, interest, or motive to lie.

title: Impeachment — Character for Truthfulness
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness’s character for truthfulness may be attacked or supported by reputation or opinion testimony.

title: Impeachment — Contradiction
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by contradiction when evidence shows that the witness’s testimony is factually incorrect.

title: Impeachment — Criminal Convictions
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by evidence of certain criminal convictions.

title: Impeachment — Prior Inconsistent Statement
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by a prior inconsistent statement if the witness is given an opportunity to explain or deny the statement.

title: Impeachment — Purpose
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Impeachment is the process of challenging the credibility of a witness.

title: Impeachment — Sensory or Mental Defects
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A witness may be impeached by showing defects in perception, memory, or narration.

title: Impeachment — Specific Instances of Conduct
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Specific instances of conduct may be inquired into on cross-examination if probative of truthfulness but may not be proven by extrinsic evidence.

title: Impeachment — Who May Impeach
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Any party, including the party that called the witness, may impeach a witness.

title: Impeachment by Criminal Conviction
subject: Evidence
ruleText: For any witness, evidence of a felony conviction must be admitted, subject to Rule 403 in a civil case or when the witness is not a criminal defendant, and subject to a stricter reverse-403 balancing when the witness is a criminal defendant. Any conviction for a crime involving a dishonest act or false statement must be admitted, regardless of punishment, subject to the ten-year limit and other narrow exceptions.

title: Imperfect Self-Defense
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: In some jurisdictions, an honest but unreasonable belief in the need for deadly self-defense reduces murder to voluntary manslaughter.

title: Impleader
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A defending party may implead a nonparty who may be liable to the defending party for all or part of the plaintiff's claim against that defending party.
buzzwords: all or part of plaintiff claim

title: Impleader — Third-Party Practice
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A defendant may implead a third party who may be liable for all or part of the plaintiff’s claim.

title: Implied Actual Authority
subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
ruleText: An agent has implied actual authority to do acts reasonably necessary to accomplish an expressly authorized task or acts customarily associated with the agent's position.

title: Implied Consent
subject: Torts
ruleText: Consent may be implied from custom, conduct, or surrounding circumstances when a reasonable person would understand the plaintiff to agree.

title: Implied Covenant of Quiet Enjoyment
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Every lease includes an implied covenant that the tenant will have quiet enjoyment without interference by the landlord.

title: Implied Covenants — Deed Delivery
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Delivery of a deed requires intent to transfer and acceptance by the grantee.

title: Implied Warranty of Authority
subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
ruleText: An agent who purports to act for a principal impliedly warrants to the third party that the agent has authority. If the agent lacks authority and the principal is not bound, the agent may be liable for breach of that implied warranty of authority.

title: Implied Warranty of Fitness for Particular Purpose
subject: Torts
ruleText: This warranty arises when the seller has reason to know the buyer's particular purpose and that the buyer is relying on the seller's skill or judgment to select suitable goods.
buzzwords: relying on seller's skill or judgment

title: Implied Warranty of Fitness for Particular Purpose
subject: Contracts
ruleText: This warranty arises when the seller has reason to know of the buyer's particular purpose and that the buyer is relying on the seller's skill or judgment to select suitable goods.
buzzwords: relying on seller's skill or judgment

title: Implied Warranty of Habitability
subject: Real Property
ruleText: In residential leases, the landlord impliedly warrants that the premises are fit for human habitation and will remain so, and the tenant may seek rent abatement, repair-and-deduct, damages, or termination for substantial breaches.

title: Implied Warranty of Habitability
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Residential leases include an implied warranty that premises are fit for human habitation.

title: Implied Warranty of Merchantability
subject: Torts
ruleText: The implied warranty of merchantability arises when a merchant sells goods and warrants that they are fit for ordinary purposes.

title: Implied Warranty of Merchantability
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The implied warranty of merchantability arises in a sale of goods if the seller is a merchant with respect to goods of that kind and warrants that the goods are fit for ordinary purposes.

title: Implied Warranty of Quality
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A builder-vendor of a new home may impliedly warrant that the home is fit and constructed in a workmanlike manner.

title: Impossibility
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Impossibility discharges contractual duties when performance becomes objectively impossible due to an unforeseen event.

title: Impossibility
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Performance is excused by impossibility when an unforeseen event occurring after contract formation makes performance objectively impossible.

title: Impossibility
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Factual impossibility is not a defense to attempt, but true legal impossibility may be.

title: Impracticability
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Performance is excused by impracticability when an unforeseen event makes performance extremely and unreasonably difficult or expensive and the event was a basic assumption of the contract.

title: Impracticability
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Impracticability discharges contractual duties when performance becomes extremely and unreasonably difficult due to an unforeseen event.

title: Improper Venue — Dismissal or Transfer
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: If venue is improper, the court must dismiss the case or transfer it to a proper venue in the interest of justice.

title: In Rem and Quasi in Rem Jurisdiction
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: In rem jurisdiction adjudicates rights in property against the world, while quasi in rem jurisdiction adjudicates rights between particular parties based on the presence of property in the forum, subject to due process limitations.

title: In Rem and Quasi In Rem Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: In rem and quasi in rem jurisdiction require minimum contacts between the defendant, the forum, and the property.

title: In-Court Identification
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An in-court identification is admissible if it has an independent source separate from any unconstitutional pretrial identification.

title: Inadvertent Disclosure
subject: Evidence
ruleText: In federal proceedings, inadvertent disclosure does not operate as a waiver if the disclosure was inadvertent, the holder of the privilege or protection took reasonable steps to prevent disclosure, and promptly took reasonable steps to rectify the error.

title: Incestuous Marriage
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A marriage between persons within a prohibited degree of consanguinity or affinity is generally void.

title: Inchoate Crimes — Definition
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Inchoate crimes punish conduct undertaken toward the commission of another crime even if the crime is not completed.

title: Incidental Damages
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Incidental damages are reasonable costs incurred in dealing with the breach, such as expenses of inspection, transportation, care, or arranging a substitute transaction.

title: Incitement
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Incitement is speech directed to producing imminent lawless action and likely to produce such action.

title: Incitement
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Advocacy is unprotected incitement only if it is directed to producing imminent lawless action and is likely to produce such action.

title: Incorporated Bill of Rights
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Most provisions of the Bill of Rights protecting individual liberties are incorporated against the states through the Fourteenth Amendment Due Process Clause.

title: Incorporation by Reference
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A separate writing may be incorporated by reference into a will if it existed at the time the will was executed, the will manifests intent to incorporate it, and the writing is sufficiently described to permit identification.

title: Indefeasibly Vested Remainder
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An indefeasibly vested remainder is certain to become possessory in the future and cannot be divested.

title: Indemnification and Reimbursement
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A partnership must indemnify a partner for liabilities incurred in the ordinary course of the partnership's business or for preservation of its business or property, and reimburse a partner for payments made and liabilities incurred in the ordinary course or for preservation of the business or property.

title: Indemnity
subject: Torts
ruleText: Indemnity shifts the entire loss from one tortfeasor to another when law or equity requires full reimbursement, such as where liability is purely vicarious.

title: Independent Basis for In-Court Identification
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An in-court identification may be admitted if it rests on an independent basis apart from the unconstitutional lineup or showup.

title: Independent Contractor Rule
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A principal is generally not vicariously liable for torts committed by an independent contractor because the principal lacks the right to control the manner and means of the contractor's work.

title: Independent Contractors
subject: Torts
ruleText: A principal is generally not vicariously liable for the torts of an independent contractor, subject to exceptions such as nondelegable duties, inherently dangerous activities, and negligent hiring.

title: Independent Source and Inevitable Discovery
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence is admissible if later obtained from an independent lawful source or if the prosecution proves it would inevitably have been discovered lawfully.

title: Independent Source Doctrine
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence is admissible if it was obtained from a source independent of the illegal conduct.

title: Indirect Revocation
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An offer is revoked indirectly when the offeree receives reliable information that the offeror has taken definite action inconsistent with an intent to enter the contract.

title: Indispensable Parties
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: If a required party cannot be joined and proceeding would be inequitable, the court must dismiss the action.

title: Inevitable Discovery Doctrine
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Evidence obtained illegally is admissible if it would have been inevitably discovered through lawful means.

title: Inherent Agency Power
subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
ruleText: Inherent agency power is an older doctrine under which a principal may be bound by certain acts of an agent that are usual or incidental to the agent's position, even absent actual authority, particularly to protect third parties dealing with an agent of an undisclosed principal.

title: Initial Aggressor
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An initial aggressor regains self-defense only by complete withdrawal communicated to the victim or if the victim escalates suddenly to deadly force.

title: Initial Disclosures
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Parties must provide initial disclosures identifying witnesses, documents, damages, and insurance without awaiting a discovery request.

title: Injunction in Personal Services Context
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Although courts generally will not order specific performance of personal services, they may sometimes enforce a valid negative covenant by injunction if equitable requirements are met.

title: Innocent Misrepresentation
subject: Torts
ruleText: Innocent misrepresentation generally does not support common law deceit damages, though it may justify rescission in contract and may trigger liability in limited contexts such as warranties.

title: Insane Delusion
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A will or provision may be invalid if it was the product of an insane delusion, meaning a false belief to which the testator adheres against all reason and evidence, and the delusion materially affected the testamentary disposition.

title: Insanity — Burden of Proof
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The defendant generally bears the burden of proving insanity by a preponderance of the evidence.

title: Insanity — Irresistible Impulse Test
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the irresistible impulse test, a defendant is not responsible if the defendant lacked the capacity to control conduct due to mental disease or defect.

title: Insanity — M'Naghten Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the M'Naghten rule, a defendant is not criminally responsible if, due to mental disease or defect, the defendant did not know the nature of the act or that the act was wrong.

title: Insanity — Model Penal Code Test
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the Model Penal Code, a defendant is not responsible if, due to mental disease or defect, the defendant lacked substantial capacity to appreciate the criminality or conform conduct to the law.

title: Inspection Rights
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Shareholders generally have the right to inspect certain corporate records, such as articles, bylaws, minutes, and accounting records, if they comply with statutory procedures and, for some records, show a proper purpose.

title: Installment Contract
subject: Contracts
ruleText: In an installment contract, the buyer may reject a nonconforming installment only if the nonconformity substantially impairs that installment and cannot be cured, and the whole contract is breached only if the nonconformity substantially impairs the value of the entire contract.

title: Installment Land Contract
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An installment land contract allows the buyer to pay the purchase price over time while the seller retains title as security until full payment, and courts often protect the buyer similarly to a mortgagor.

title: Instrument
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: An instrument is a negotiable instrument or another writing that evidences a right to payment of a monetary obligation, is not itself a security agreement or lease, and is of a type that in ordinary course is transferred by delivery with any necessary endorsement or assignment.

title: Insufficient Process and Insufficient Service of Process
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Rule 12(b)(4) challenges defects in process itself, while Rule 12(b)(5) challenges the manner or sufficiency of service of process.

title: Integrated Agreements
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An integrated agreement is a final written expression of one or more terms of an agreement.

title: Integration of a Will
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Integration allows all papers present at execution that the testator intended to be part of the will to be treated together as one will.

title: Intended Killing
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An intentional killing without adequate mitigation, excuse, or justification is murder.

title: Intended vs Incidental Beneficiaries
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Only an intended beneficiary has enforceable rights under a contract, while an incidental beneficiary has no enforceable rights.

title: Intended vs Incidental Beneficiaries
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Only an intended beneficiary, not an incidental beneficiary, may enforce the contract.

title: Intent
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intent exists when the defendant acts with the purpose to cause the consequences of the act or knows with substantial certainty that those consequences will result.

title: Intent — Definition
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intent exists when a person acts with the purpose to cause a result or with knowledge that the result is substantially certain to occur.

title: Intent — General Intent
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: General intent crimes require only that the defendant intended to perform the prohibited act.

title: Intent — Specific Intent
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Specific intent crimes require that the defendant intended to bring about a particular result.

title: Intent to Create a Trust
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: The settlor must manifest present intent to impose enforceable fiduciary duties on a trustee to hold property for beneficiaries.

title: Intent to Inflict Serious Bodily Injury
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A killing caused by conduct intended to inflict serious bodily injury is murder even if death was not specifically intended.

title: Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intentional infliction of emotional distress occurs when a defendant intentionally or recklessly engages in extreme and outrageous conduct causing severe emotional distress.

title: Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress
subject: Torts
ruleText: IIED requires extreme and outrageous conduct that intentionally or recklessly causes the plaintiff severe emotional distress.

title: Intentional Interference with Business Expectancy
subject: Torts
ruleText: This tort requires a valid business expectancy, defendant's knowledge, intentional interference by improper means or improper purpose, causation, and damages.

title: Intentional Interference with Contract
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intentional interference with contract occurs when a defendant intentionally induces a third party to breach an existing contract causing damages.

title: Intentional Interference with Contract
subject: Torts
ruleText: This tort requires a valid contract, defendant's knowledge of the contract, intentional inducement of breach, actual breach or disruption, and resulting damages.

title: Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intentional interference with prospective economic advantage occurs when a defendant intentionally interferes with an expected economic relationship through wrongful conduct.

title: Intentional Misrepresentation (Fraud)
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intentional misrepresentation occurs when a defendant knowingly makes a false statement of material fact with intent to induce reliance and the plaintiff justifiably relies and suffers damages.

title: Intentional Nuisance
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intentional nuisance occurs when the defendant knows or is substantially certain that the conduct will result in interference with land use.

title: Interest Analysis in Torts
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Under governmental interest analysis, the court identifies the policies behind the competing state laws and determines which state has a legitimate interest in having its law applied to the dispute.

title: Interested Witness
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: At common law, a gift to an interested witness could be void unless there were sufficient disinterested witnesses, but many modern statutes purge only the excess gift or abolish the rule entirely.

title: Interests in Personal Property
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Issues concerning tangible personal property are often governed by the law of the state where the property is located at the relevant time, though the applicable rule may depend on the issue and forum methodology.

title: Interlocutory Appeals
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Certain interlocutory orders may be appealed before final judgment, including orders regarding injunctions and other orders made immediately appealable by statute or recognized exception.

title: Intermediate Scrutiny
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Under intermediate scrutiny, the government must show the classification is substantially related to an important governmental interest.

title: Intermediate Scrutiny — Gender
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Classifications based on gender are subject to intermediate scrutiny.

title: Intermediate Scrutiny — Illegitimacy
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Classifications based on illegitimacy are subject to intermediate scrutiny.

title: Intermediate Scrutiny — Standard
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Under intermediate scrutiny, the government must show the law is substantially related to an important government interest.

title: Interpleader
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Interpleader allows a stakeholder holding property claimed by multiple claimants to join them in one action to avoid multiple liability or inconsistent obligations.

title: Interpleader — Rule Interpleader
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Rule interpleader allows a stakeholder exposed to multiple liability to join all claimants in one action.

title: Interpleader — Statutory Interpleader
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Statutory interpleader requires minimal diversity, an amount in controversy of $500, and deposit of the disputed property.

title: Interrogation — Definition
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Interrogation includes express questioning or its functional equivalent likely to elicit an incriminating response.

title: Interrogation for Miranda
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Interrogation includes express questioning and words or actions police should know are reasonably likely to elicit an incriminating response.

title: Interrogatories — Limits
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A party may serve up to 25 interrogatories unless the court permits more.

title: Intervening Cause
subject: Torts
ruleText: An intervening cause is an event that occurs after the defendant’s conduct and contributes to the plaintiff’s injury.

title: Intervention — As of Right
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Intervention as of right is permitted when the applicant claims an interest that may be impaired and is not adequately represented by existing parties.

title: Intervention — Permissive
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Permissive intervention is allowed when the applicant’s claim or defense shares a common question of law or fact with the main action.
buzzwords: common question of law or fact

title: Intervention of Right
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Intervention of right is available when the applicant claims an interest relating to the property or transaction, disposition may impair that interest, and existing parties do not adequately represent the interest.

title: Intestate Succession
subject: Trusts
ruleText: If a decedent dies intestate, property passes according to the state intestacy statute.

title: Intoxication — Involuntary
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Involuntary intoxication is a complete defense if it negates the required mens rea.

title: Intoxication — Voluntary
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Voluntary intoxication is a defense to specific intent crimes but not to general intent crimes.

title: Intrusion Upon Seclusion
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intrusion upon seclusion is an intentional intrusion into the plaintiff's private affairs in a manner highly offensive to a reasonable person.

title: Intrusion Upon Seclusion
subject: Torts
ruleText: Intrusion upon seclusion occurs when a defendant intentionally intrudes upon a plaintiff’s private affairs in a highly offensive manner.

title: Invasion of Privacy — Overview
subject: Torts
ruleText: Invasion of privacy consists of four distinct torts: intrusion upon seclusion, appropriation, false light, and public disclosure of private facts.

title: Inventory
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Inventory is goods, other than farm products, that are leased by a person, held for sale or lease, furnished under a contract of service, consist of raw materials, work in process, or materials used or consumed in a business in a short period of time.

title: Inventory Search
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may conduct a warrantless inventory search of lawfully impounded property pursuant to standardized procedures.

title: Inventory Searches
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may conduct a routine inventory search of lawfully impounded property pursuant to standardized procedures.

title: Investment Property
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Investment property includes certificated securities, uncertificated securities, security entitlements, securities accounts, commodity contracts, and commodity accounts.

title: Invocation of Counsel Under Miranda
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: If the suspect unambiguously requests counsel, interrogation must cease until counsel is present, unless the suspect reinitiates communication or there is a sufficient break in custody.

title: Invocation of Right to Counsel
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: If a suspect invokes the right to counsel, all interrogation must cease until counsel is provided.

title: Invocation of Right to Silence
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: If a suspect invokes the right to remain silent, interrogation must cease.

title: Invocation of Silence
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: If the suspect unambiguously invokes the right to remain silent, police must scrupulously honor the invocation before attempting renewed questioning.

title: Involuntary Intoxication
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Involuntary intoxication is a defense if it renders the defendant unable to appreciate criminality or conform conduct, similar to insanity.

title: Involuntary Manslaughter
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Involuntary manslaughter is an unintentional killing resulting from criminal negligence or during an unlawful act not amounting to felony murder.

title: Involuntary Manslaughter — Criminal Negligence
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Involuntary manslaughter occurs when a death results from criminal negligence.

title: Involuntary Manslaughter — Misdemeanor Manslaughter
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Involuntary manslaughter occurs when a death results from the commission of a non-dangerous misdemeanor.

title: Irrelevant Evidence
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Irrelevant evidence is not admissible.

title: Irresistible Impulse
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under this test, a defendant is insane if mental illness prevented self-control and made the defendant unable to conform conduct to the law.

title: Irrevocable Trust
subject: Trusts
ruleText: An irrevocable trust cannot be modified without beneficiary consent or court approval.

title: Issue Preclusion
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Issue preclusion bars relitigation of an issue when the same issue was actually litigated, actually decided, essential to a valid final judgment, and asserted against a party who had a full and fair opportunity to litigate.

title: Issue Preclusion — Criminal Convictions
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A criminal conviction may have issue preclusive effect in subsequent civil litigation.

title: Issue Preclusion — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Issue preclusion bars relitigation of an issue that was actually litigated and necessarily decided in a prior action.

title: Issue Preclusion — Mutuality
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Issue preclusion does not require mutuality, allowing nonparties to assert it defensively or offensively.

title: Issue Preclusion — Requirements
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Issue preclusion requires the same issue, actually litigated, essential to the judgment, and asserted against a party to the prior action.

title: Joinder of Parties — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: The Federal Rules permit joinder of parties and claims to promote efficiency and avoid multiple lawsuits.

title: Joint and Several Liability
subject: Torts
ruleText: Under joint and several liability, each liable defendant may be responsible for the entire indivisible harm, subject to contribution or indemnity rights.

title: Joint Tenancy
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A joint tenancy is a concurrent estate with right of survivorship that traditionally requires the four unities of time, title, interest, and possession.

title: Joint Tenancy
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A joint tenancy requires the four unities of time, title, interest, and possession and includes a right of survivorship.

title: Judge and Juror as Witnesses
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The presiding judge may not testify as a witness at the trial, and a juror may not testify as a witness before the other jurors at the trial. A party need not object to preserve the issue as to the judge.

title: Judge-Made Federal Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: When no Federal Rule applies, federal courts balance state and federal interests to determine whether to apply state law.

title: Judgment as a Matter of Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may grant judgment as a matter of law when a reasonable jury would not have a legally sufficient evidentiary basis to find for the nonmoving party.
buzzwords: judgment as a matter of law

title: Judgment as a Matter of Law — JMOL
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Judgment as a matter of law may be granted when a reasonable jury would not have a legally sufficient evidentiary basis to find for the nonmoving party.
buzzwords: judgment as a matter of law

title: Judgment on the Merits
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Claim preclusion generally requires a valid final judgment on the merits, meaning a judgment that finally resolves the claim in a manner treated as merits based under preclusion law.

title: Judicial Dissolution
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A court may order dissolution in cases such as deadlock, waste, fraud, illegality, or oppression of minority owners, depending on the entity type and statute.

title: Judicial Notice
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A court may judicially notice a fact that is not subject to reasonable dispute because it is either generally known within the trial court's territorial jurisdiction or can be accurately and readily determined from sources whose accuracy cannot reasonably be questioned.

title: Judicial Notice — Adjudicative Facts
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A court may take judicial notice of an adjudicative fact that is not subject to reasonable dispute.

title: Judicial Notice — Civil vs Criminal Cases
subject: Evidence
ruleText: In a civil case, the jury must accept a judicially noticed fact as conclusive, while in a criminal case the jury may accept or reject it.

title: Judicial Notice — Sources
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A fact may be judicially noticed if it is generally known or can be accurately and readily determined from reliable sources.

title: Judicial Power — Federal Courts
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The judicial power of the United States is vested in the Supreme Court and inferior federal courts established by Congress.

title: Judicial Review
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Judicial review is the power of the federal courts to review the constitutionality of legislative and executive actions.

title: Judicial Review
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The Supreme Court has authority to review the constitutionality of acts of Congress and executive actions and may invalidate governmental action that conflicts with the Constitution.

title: Judicial Review of Executive Action
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Federal courts may review executive actions for constitutionality and compliance with federal law.

title: Jurisdiction to Grant Divorce
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: A state may grant a divorce if at least one spouse is domiciled there, and a divorce decree based on proper domicile is generally entitled to full faith and credit.

title: Juror Testimony to Impeach Verdict
subject: Evidence
ruleText: During an inquiry into the validity of a verdict or indictment, a juror generally may not testify about statements made or incidents that occurred during deliberations, the effect of anything on a juror's vote, or a juror's mental processes. But a juror may testify about whether extraneous prejudicial information was improperly brought to the jury's attention, whether an outside influence was improperly brought to bear on a juror, or whether there was a mistake in entering the verdict on the verdict form.

title: Jury Demand and Waiver
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A party generally must timely demand a jury trial or the right is waived.

title: Jury Pool — Fair Cross-Section
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Jury pools must represent a fair cross-section of the community.

title: Jury Verdict — General Verdict
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A general verdict states which party prevails and the amount of damages, if any.

title: Jury Verdict — General Verdict with Interrogatories
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A general verdict with interrogatories allows the jury to return a general verdict and answer written questions.

title: Jury Verdict — Special Verdict
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A special verdict requires the jury to make written findings on specific factual issues.

title: Just Compensation
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Just compensation is measured by the fair market value of the property at the time of the taking.

title: Just Compensation
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Just compensation is usually the fair market value of the property at the time of the taking.

title: Juvenile Adjudications
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of a juvenile adjudication is generally not admissible to impeach, but in a criminal case the court may allow it against a witness other than the accused if an adult conviction for that offense would be admissible and admitting the evidence is necessary to fairly determine guilt or innocence.

title: Kidnapping
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Kidnapping is unlawful confinement plus movement of the victim or concealment in a secret place.

title: Kidnapping
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Kidnapping is the unlawful confinement of a person involving movement or concealment that increases the risk of harm.

title: Killings by Third Parties
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Under the majority agency approach, felony murder applies only to killings by the felon or co-felon, while some jurisdictions use a proximate-cause approach for foreseeable third-party killings.

title: Knock-and-Announce — Exceptions
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The knock-and-announce requirement is excused when officers reasonably believe it would be dangerous, futile, or result in destruction of evidence.

title: Knock-and-Announce Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police generally must knock and announce their presence before executing a warrant unless an exception applies.

title: Land Contracts
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Contracts for the sale of land or interests in land generally fall within the Statute of Frauds.

title: Landlord Consent to Transfer
subject: Real Property
ruleText: If the lease requires landlord consent before transfer, the landlord may withhold consent, but in many jurisdictions residential and commercial leases imply a reasonableness requirement unless the lease gives absolute discretion.

title: Landlord Duties — Deliver Possession
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A landlord must deliver actual possession of the premises at the start of the lease.

title: Landlord Liability
subject: Torts
ruleText: A landlord may be liable for common areas under landlord control, hidden defects known at lease inception, negligent repairs, and defects existing at the time of lease for public use.

title: Landlord Remedies — Tenant Breach
subject: Real Property
ruleText: If a tenant breaches, the landlord may evict, sue for rent, or mitigate damages.

title: Lapse
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A devise lapses if the beneficiary fails to survive the testator, unless an anti-lapse statute or another doctrine saves the gift.

title: Larceny
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Larceny is the trespassory taking and carrying away of the tangible personal property of another with intent to permanently deprive.

title: Larceny
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Larceny is the trespassory taking and carrying away of the personal property of another with intent to permanently deprive.

title: Larceny — Continuing Trespass
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Larceny may be established by continuing trespass when the defendant initially takes property without intent but later forms intent to permanently deprive.

title: Larceny by Trick
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Larceny by trick occurs when possession is obtained through fraud with intent to permanently deprive.

title: Larceny by Trick
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Larceny by trick is obtaining possession of property by fraud with intent to permanently deprive, where title does not pass.

title: Last Clear Chance
subject: Torts
ruleText: Under the last clear chance doctrine, a contributorily negligent plaintiff may still recover if the defendant had the last clear opportunity to avoid the harm but failed to do so.

title: Lateral Support
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A landowner has a right to lateral support from adjoining land in its natural state, and an adjoining owner may be strictly liable for removing that support and causing collapse of natural land.

title: Law of the Situs for Real Property
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Issues concerning title to, transfer of, and interests in real property are generally governed by the law of the situs, the state where the land is located.

title: Laws Targeting Religion
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A law that targets religion, discriminates among religions, or creates a system of individualized exemptions hostile to religion is subject to strict scrutiny.

title: Lay Opinion
subject: Evidence
