ruleText: The Seventh Amendment preserves the right to a jury trial in federal civil actions for legal claims, but not for purely equitable claims.

title: Severance of Joint Tenancy
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A joint tenancy is severed when one joint tenant destroys one of the four unities, such as by conveying that tenant's interest to a third party.

title: Severance of Joint Tenancy
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A joint tenancy is severed when one co-tenant transfers their interest, destroying the unities.

title: Sex Classifications
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Sex classifications are subject to intermediate scrutiny and require an exceedingly persuasive justification.

title: Shareholder Class Actions
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A shareholder class action may be appropriate when shareholders suffer a common direct injury, such as misleading proxy disclosures affecting voting or transactions impacting shareholders in the same direct manner.

title: Shareholder Meetings
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Corporations generally must hold annual shareholder meetings, and special meetings may be called by persons authorized by statute, the articles, or bylaws. Notice must state the time and place and, for special meetings, often the purpose.

title: Shareholder Quorum and Voting
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A majority of the votes entitled to be cast on a matter generally constitutes a quorum for shareholder action, and if a quorum exists, action is approved if the votes cast in favor exceed votes cast against, unless the articles, bylaws, or statute require a greater vote.
buzzwords: majority of votes entitled to be cast

title: Shareholder Voting Agreements
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Shareholder voting agreements are generally valid and specifically enforceable if they do not violate statute or public policy.

title: Shareholders — Derivative Actions
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A shareholder may bring a derivative action on behalf of the corporation after making a demand on the board or showing demand futility.

title: Shareholders — Voting and Management
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Shareholders elect directors and vote on fundamental changes but do not manage daily corporate affairs.

title: Shares of Descendants
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Descendants take the balance of the intestate estate by representation under the applicable per stirpes, modern per stirpes, or per capita at each generation system.

title: Shelter Rule
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A transferee from a bona fide purchaser takes shelter in the transferor's status and receives the same priority, even if the transferee had notice.
buzzwords: transferee from a bona fide purchaser

title: Shelter Rule
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Under the shelter rule, a transferee takes the same rights as the transferor.

title: Shifting and Springing Executory Interests
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A shifting executory interest divests another transferee, while a springing executory interest divests the grantor or follows a gap in possession.

title: Shopkeeper's Privilege
subject: Torts
ruleText: A shopkeeper has a limited privilege to detain a suspected thief for a reasonable time in a reasonable manner when there is a reasonable belief theft occurred.

title: Signature by Another on Testator's Behalf
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A will may be signed by another person on the testator's behalf if done at the testator's direction and in the testator's conscious presence or as otherwise allowed by statute.

title: Significant-Connection Jurisdiction
subject: Family Law
ruleText: If no state qualifies as the home state or the home state declines jurisdiction, a state may exercise jurisdiction when the child and at least one parent have a significant connection with the state beyond mere physical presence and substantial evidence concerning the child's care, protection, training, and relationships is available there.

title: Silence as Acceptance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Silence ordinarily is not acceptance unless the offeree takes benefits with reason to know compensation is expected, the parties so agree, or prior dealings make silence reasonable.

title: Sixth Amendment — Right to Counsel
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to counsel in all criminal prosecutions after the initiation of adversarial judicial proceedings.

title: Slayer Rule
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A person who feloniously and intentionally kills the decedent is barred from taking from the decedent's estate, and the estate is distributed as though the killer predeceased the decedent.

title: Social Host Liability
subject: Torts
ruleText: A social host generally is not liable for serving alcohol to adult guests, absent a statute imposing liability.

title: Solicitation
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Solicitation is asking, enticing, counseling, or commanding another to commit a crime with the intent that the crime be committed.

title: Solicitation — Completion
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Solicitation is complete once the request or encouragement is made, regardless of whether the crime is committed.

title: Solicitation — Definition
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Solicitation occurs when a defendant intentionally invites, requests, or encourages another to commit a crime.

title: Source of Erie Doctrine
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: The Erie doctrine requires federal courts to apply state substantive law to prevent forum shopping and inequitable administration of the laws.

title: Sources of Legal Duty
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Legal duties arise from statute, contract, special relationship, voluntary assumption of care that secludes the victim, or creation of the peril.

title: Special Damages
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Special damages must be specifically stated.

title: Special Litigation Committee
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A corporation may appoint a special litigation committee of disinterested directors or others authorized by statute to investigate whether pursuing a derivative claim is in the corporation's best interests, and its recommendation may support dismissal if statutory standards are satisfied.

title: Special Needs Searches
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Warrantless searches may be conducted without probable cause when special needs beyond law enforcement make the search reasonable.

title: Special Needs Searches
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Searches conducted for needs beyond ordinary law enforcement may be reasonable without individualized suspicion under balancing analysis.

title: Special Relationships
subject: Torts
ruleText: Special relationships such as common carrier-passenger, innkeeper-guest, business-invitee, school-student, employer-employee, or custodian-dependent may create a duty to aid, protect, or control.

title: Special Warranty Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A special warranty deed warrants against title defects arising only through the grantor, not defects existing before the grantor held title.

title: Special Warranty Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A special warranty deed provides warranties only against defects arising during the grantor’s ownership.

title: Specific Acts on Cross-Examination
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Except for criminal convictions under Rule 609, extrinsic evidence is not admissible to prove specific instances of a witness's conduct in order to attack or support the witness's character for truthfulness. But on cross-examination the court may allow inquiry into specific instances if they are probative of the character for truthfulness or untruthfulness of the witness or another witness whose character the witness being cross-examined has testified about.

title: Specific Intent Crimes
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A specific intent crime requires the defendant to act with a particular objective beyond the actus reus itself.

title: Specific Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Specific jurisdiction exists when the defendant purposefully avails itself of the forum and the claim arises out of or relates to the defendant's forum contacts.

title: Specific Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Specific jurisdiction exists when the plaintiff’s claim arises out of or relates to the defendant’s contacts with the forum.

title: Specific Performance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Specific performance is available when monetary damages are inadequate to compensate the non-breaching party.

title: Specific Performance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Specific performance is available when legal damages are inadequate, such as for unique goods or real property, and equitable relief is otherwise appropriate.

title: Specific Performance — Land Contracts
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Specific performance is commonly available for contracts involving land because land is considered unique.

title: Specific Performance — Personal Services
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Specific performance is not available for contracts involving personal services.

title: Speedy Trial
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Courts balance length of delay, reason for delay, defendant's assertion of the right, and prejudice to the defendant.

title: Speedy Trial — Barker v. Wingo Factors
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Speedy trial claims are evaluated by balancing length of delay, reason for delay, defendant’s assertion of the right, and prejudice to the defendant.

title: Speedy Trial — Right
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: The Sixth Amendment guarantees the right to a speedy trial.

title: Spending Power — Conditions
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may attach conditions to federal funding if the conditions are related to the purpose of the spending and are not unduly coercive.

title: Spending Power and Conditional Grants
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may condition federal grants on compliance with federal requirements if the spending is for the general welfare, conditions are unambiguous, related to the program, not independently unconstitutional, and not unduly coercive.

title: Spendthrift Trust
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A spendthrift trust restricts both voluntary and involuntary transfer of beneficiary interests.

title: Spendthrift Trust
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A spendthrift clause restrains both voluntary and involuntary transfer of a beneficiary's interest, preventing the beneficiary from assigning it and generally preventing most creditors from reaching it before distribution.

title: Spousal Communications Privilege
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The spousal communications privilege protects confidential communications made between spouses during the marriage.

title: Spousal Privilege — Testimonial
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The spousal testimonial privilege allows a spouse to refuse to testify against the other spouse in a criminal case.

title: Spousal Support (Alimony)
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Spousal support may be awarded and modified based on factors including need, ability to pay, duration of marriage, and a substantial change in circumstances.

title: Spousal Support Factors
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Courts consider factors such as need, ability to pay, duration of the marriage, standard of living during marriage, age and health, earning capacities, contributions to the marriage, and the time needed for education or training.

title: Spousal Testimonial Privilege
subject: Evidence
ruleText: In criminal cases, the witness-spouse may refuse to testify adversely against the defendant-spouse during the marriage. The privilege belongs to the witness-spouse and ends upon divorce.

title: Standard of Care — Children
subject: Torts
ruleText: A child is held to the standard of a reasonable child of the same age, intelligence, and experience unless engaged in an adult activity.

title: Standard of Care — Professionals
subject: Torts
ruleText: A professional is held to the standard of care of a reasonably competent professional in the same field.

title: Standard of Care — Reasonable Person
subject: Torts
ruleText: The standard of care is that of a reasonable person under the same or similar circumstances.

title: Standing
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A plaintiff must show a concrete and particularized injury in fact, causation, and redressability.

title: Standing — Causation
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Causation requires that the injury be fairly traceable to the challenged conduct.

title: Standing — General Rule
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: To have standing, a plaintiff must show injury in fact, causation, and redressability.

title: Standing — Injury in Fact
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: An injury in fact must be concrete, particularized, and actual or imminent.

title: Standing — No Vicarious Standing
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A defendant may not challenge a search or seizure based solely on the violation of another person’s rights.

title: Standing — Personal Rights
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A defendant may invoke the exclusionary rule only if the defendant’s own Fourth Amendment rights were violated.

title: Standing — Public Nuisance
subject: Torts
ruleText: A private individual may recover for public nuisance only if the individual suffers a special injury different in kind from the general public.

title: Standing — Redressability
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Redressability requires that a favorable court decision is likely to remedy the injury.

title: Standing to Challenge Search
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Only a person whose own reasonable expectation of privacy was violated may seek suppression under the Fourth Amendment.

title: Standing to Contest a Will
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A person has standing to contest a will if the person has a pecuniary interest that would be affected by the probate of the challenged instrument, such as an intestate heir or beneficiary under an earlier will.

title: State Action Requirement
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Constitutional protections generally apply only to governmental action, not purely private conduct, unless a recognized state action doctrine makes the private conduct fairly attributable to the state.

title: State Action Requirement
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The Equal Protection Clause applies only to government action and not to purely private conduct.

title: State Action Requirement — Speech
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The First Amendment applies only to government action and not to private conduct.

title: State Alienage Classifications
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: State classifications based on alienage usually receive strict scrutiny, except for classifications concerning democratic self-government and political function positions, which receive rational basis.

title: State Officers and Personal Liability
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: State officers may be sued in their personal capacity for damages, subject to personal immunities, because such suits are not treated as suits against the state.

title: State Police Power
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: States possess police power to regulate for the health, safety, welfare, and morals of their citizens.

title: State Substantive Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: State substantive law includes elements of claims, statutes of limitations, and rules governing damages.

title: Statement — Definition
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement is a person’s oral assertion, written assertion, or nonverbal conduct intended as an assertion.

title: Statement Against Interest
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement against interest is admissible when a declarant makes a statement so contrary to the declarant’s pecuniary, proprietary, or penal interest that a reasonable person would not have made it unless true.

title: Statement Against Interest
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement that a reasonable person in the declarant's position would have made only if the person believed it to be true because, when made, it was so contrary to the declarant's proprietary or pecuniary interest or had so great a tendency to invalidate the declarant's claim or to expose the declarant to civil or criminal liability is admissible if the declarant is unavailable. In a criminal case, a statement tending to expose the declarant to criminal liability and offered to exculpate the accused requires corroborating circumstances that clearly indicate trustworthiness.

title: Statement Against Interest — Corroboration
subject: Evidence
ruleText: In criminal cases, a statement against penal interest offered to exculpate the accused must be supported by corroborating circumstances indicating trustworthiness.

title: Statement of an Opposing Party
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement is defined as not hearsay if it is offered against an opposing party and was made by the party in an individual or representative capacity; is one the party manifested that it adopted or believed true; was made by a person whom the party authorized to make a statement on the subject; was made by the party's agent or employee on a matter within the scope of that relationship and while it existed; or was made by the party's coconspirator during and in furtherance of the conspiracy.

title: Statement of Personal or Family History
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Statements concerning a declarant’s own birth, marriage, divorce, ancestry, or similar family history are admissible when the declarant is unavailable.

title: Statement of Personal or Family History
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement about the declarant's own birth, adoption, legitimacy, ancestry, marriage, divorce, relationship by blood, adoption, or marriage, or similar facts of personal or family history, and certain statements about another person concerning such matters if the declarant was related to or closely associated with the person, are admissible if the declarant is unavailable.

title: Statements for Medical Diagnosis or Treatment
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement made for, and reasonably pertinent to, medical diagnosis or treatment that describes medical history, past or present symptoms, their inception, or their general cause is admissible.

title: Statements for Medical Diagnosis or Treatment
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Statements made for purposes of medical diagnosis or treatment describing medical history, symptoms, or cause are admissible.

title: Statute of Frauds
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Certain contracts are unenforceable unless evidenced by a writing signed by the party to be charged.
buzzwords: signed by the party to be charged

title: Statute of Frauds
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A contract for the sale of an interest in land must generally be in writing, signed by the party to be charged, and contain the essential terms.

title: Statute of Frauds — Covered Contracts (MYLEGS)
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds applies to contracts for marriage, contracts that cannot be performed within one year, interests in land, executor promises, guarantees, and contracts for the sale of goods priced at $500 or more.

title: Statute of Frauds — Equitable Estoppel
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A party may be estopped from asserting the Statute of Frauds when reliance makes enforcement necessary to prevent injustice.

title: Statute of Frauds — Essential Terms
subject: Real Property
ruleText: The writing must identify the parties, describe the land, and state the price.

title: Statute of Frauds — General Rule
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Certain contracts must be in writing and signed by the party to be charged to be enforceable.

title: Statute of Frauds — Interest in Land
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds applies to contracts creating or transferring an interest in land.

title: Statute of Frauds — Land Contracts
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A contract for the sale of land must be in writing and signed by the party to be charged.

title: Statute of Frauds — Main Purpose Exception
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds does not apply to a suretyship promise when the promisor’s main purpose is to serve the promisor’s own economic interest.

title: Statute of Frauds — One-Year Provision
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The one-year provision applies only to contracts that cannot be fully performed within one year from the time of formation.

title: Statute of Frauds — Signature Requirement
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds requires a signature by the party against whom enforcement is sought.
buzzwords: party against whom enforcement is sought

title: Statute of Frauds — Suretyship (Guarantee)
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds applies to a promise to answer for the debt or duty of another.

title: Statute of Frauds — Writing Requirements
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The writing must reasonably identify the subject matter, indicate that a contract has been made, and state the essential terms with reasonable certainty.

title: Statutes of Limitation
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Traditionally, statutes of limitation were treated as procedural and governed by forum law, but many jurisdictions use borrowing statutes or treat certain limitation rules as substantive in particular contexts.

title: Statutory Period
subject: Real Property
ruleText: The statutory period for adverse possession is set by state law.

title: Statutory Rape
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statutory rape is sexual intercourse with a person below the age of consent, and mistake as to age is often not a defense.

title: Statutory Rape
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statutory rape is sexual intercourse with a person under the age of consent regardless of consent or mistake of age.

title: Statutory Right of Redemption
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Some states allow redemption after foreclosure by statute.

title: Stop and Frisk — Frisk
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may frisk a detainee if they reasonably suspect the person is armed and dangerous.

title: Stop and Frisk — Terry Stop
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may briefly detain a person if they have reasonable suspicion of criminal activity.

title: Strict Foreclosure
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A secured party may accept collateral in full or partial satisfaction of the obligation if the debtor consents after default and other statutory conditions are met, including sending proposal notices to certain interested parties and receiving no timely objection. In consumer transactions, acceptance in partial satisfaction is generally not allowed.

title: Strict Liability
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A strict liability crime requires no mens rea as to one or more elements and commonly appears in public-welfare or statutory rape contexts.

title: Strict Liability — General Rule
subject: Torts
ruleText: Strict liability applies when a defendant engages in certain activities and is liable for resulting harm regardless of the level of care exercised.
buzzwords: regardless of the level of care

title: Strict Liability Crimes
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Strict liability crimes require no mens rea and typically involve public welfare offenses.

title: Strict Liability Nuisance
subject: Torts
ruleText: Strict liability nuisance occurs when interference results from abnormally dangerous activities.

title: Strict Per Stirpes
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: Under strict per stirpes, the estate is divided into shares at the first generational level below the decedent regardless of whether anyone there survives, and descendants of a deceased child take that child's share by representation.

title: Strict Products Liability
subject: Torts
ruleText: The plaintiff must show the defendant is a commercial supplier, the product was defective, the defect existed when it left the defendant's control, and the defect actually and proximately caused the plaintiff's injury while the product was being used in a foreseeable manner.

title: Strict Products Liability — Elements
subject: Torts
ruleText: Strict products liability requires that the product was defective, the defect existed when the product left the defendant’s control, and the defect caused injury.

title: Strict Scrutiny
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Under strict scrutiny, the government must show the law is narrowly tailored to achieve a compelling governmental interest.

title: Strict Scrutiny — Fundamental Rights
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Strict scrutiny applies when a law burdens a fundamental right such as voting, interstate travel, or access to the courts.

title: Strict Scrutiny — Standard
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Under strict scrutiny, the government must show the law is narrowly tailored to achieve a compelling government interest.

title: Strict Scrutiny — Suspect Classifications
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Strict scrutiny applies to classifications based on race, national origin, and alienage.

title: Subjacent Support
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An owner of surface land has a right to subjacent support from the underlying strata, and one who withdraws that support may be liable for resulting damage.

title: Subject Matter Jurisdiction — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts have subject matter jurisdiction only if authorized by the Constitution and statute.

title: Sublease
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A sublease transfers less than the tenant’s entire remaining interest.

title: Subscriptions for Shares
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A preincorporation subscription is an offer to buy stock in the future corporation and is often irrevocable for the period specified by statute unless all subscribers agree to revoke.

title: Subsequent Illegality
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract is discharged when performance becomes illegal due to a change in law after formation.

title: Subsequent Remedial Measures
subject: Evidence
subtopic: Relevance and Reasons for Excluding Relevant Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of measures that would have made an earlier injury or harm less likely to occur is not admissible to prove negligence, culpable conduct, a defect in a product or its design, or a need for a warning or instruction. But it may be admissible for another purpose, such as impeachment or proving ownership, control, or the feasibility of precautionary measures if disputed.

title: Subsequent Remedial Measures — Permitted Uses
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of subsequent remedial measures may be admitted to prove ownership, control, feasibility, or impeachment.

title: Subsequent Similar Accidents
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Evidence of subsequent accidents may be admissible to prove a dangerous condition, causation, or notice if sufficiently similar and not barred by some specific exclusion; unlike subsequent remedial measures, subsequent accidents are not categorically excluded.

title: Substance Over Form
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Article 9 looks to the economic substance of a transaction rather than its label, and a transaction that in substance creates a security interest will be governed as a secured transaction even if the parties label it as a lease, consignment, sale, or another arrangement.

title: Substance Versus Procedure
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: The forum generally applies its own procedural rules, but it may apply another state's substantive law when choice-of-law rules point to that law.

title: Substantial Effects on Interstate Commerce
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may regulate economic or commercial intrastate activity that, in the aggregate, substantially affects interstate commerce.

title: Substantial Performance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Substantial performance occurs when a party performs in good faith and any breach is minor rather than material.

title: Substantial Performance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under common law, substantial performance satisfies the contract and triggers the other party's duty to perform, subject to damages for the defects.

title: Substantive Due Process — General Rule
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Substantive due process limits the government’s ability to infringe on fundamental rights regardless of the procedures used.

title: Substantive Due Process — Non-Fundamental Rights
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Laws affecting non-fundamental rights are upheld if they are rationally related to a legitimate government interest.

title: Substantive Due Process — Standard of Review
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Laws infringing fundamental rights are subject to strict scrutiny.

title: Succession to Personal Property
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Succession to a decedent's personal property is generally governed by the law of the decedent's domicile at death.

title: Succession to Real Property
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Succession to real property is generally governed by the law of the situs, the state where the land is located.

title: Suggestive Identification Procedures
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An identification procedure violates due process if it is unnecessarily suggestive and creates a substantial likelihood of misidentification.

title: Summary Judgment
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Summary judgment is proper when there is no genuine dispute of material fact and the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law.
buzzwords: judgment as a matter of law

title: Summary Judgment — Burden of Proof
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: The moving party must show the absence of a genuine issue of material fact.

title: Summary Judgment — Evidence
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Courts consider pleadings, depositions, documents, affidavits, and admissions when deciding summary judgment.

title: Summary Judgment — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Summary judgment is granted when there is no genuine dispute of material fact and the movant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law.
buzzwords: entitled to judgment as a matter of law

title: Superseding Cause
subject: Torts
ruleText: A superseding cause breaks the chain of causation when the intervening act is unforeseeable and relieves the defendant of liability.

title: Superseding Cause
subject: Torts
ruleText: An intervening cause becomes superseding when it is so unforeseeable that it breaks the chain of proximate causation and relieves the defendant of liability for subsequent harm.

title: Suppl. Jurisdiction — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts may exercise supplemental jurisdiction over claims that form part of the same case or controversy.

title: Supplemental Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Supplemental jurisdiction exists when the additional claim and the original claim arise from a common nucleus of operative fact such that they form part of the same case or controversy.

title: Supplemental Jurisdiction — Discretionary Decline
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may decline supplemental jurisdiction if the claim raises novel state issues, substantially predominates, or if the original claims are dismissed.

title: Supplemental Jurisdiction — Diversity Limitation
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: In diversity cases, supplemental jurisdiction does not allow claims by plaintiffs that would destroy complete diversity.

title: Supplemental Jurisdiction — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal courts may exercise supplemental jurisdiction over claims that form part of the same case or controversy.

title: Supplemental Jurisdiction Limitation in Diversity Cases
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: In diversity cases, supplemental jurisdiction generally cannot be used to override complete diversity for certain claims by plaintiffs against persons made parties under the federal joinder rules.

title: Support and Visitation Are Independent
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A parent's duty to pay child support is independent of the other parent's duty to permit visitation, so denial of visitation generally does not excuse support payments, and nonpayment does not justify denying visitation absent court order.
buzzwords: support not excused by denied visitation

title: Support Trust
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A support trust directs the trustee to make distributions as necessary for the beneficiary's support, education, health, or maintenance, and the beneficiary may compel reasonable distributions within that standard.

title: Supremacy Clause
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The Supremacy Clause provides that federal law is the supreme law of the land and prevails over conflicting state law.

title: Surrender
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Surrender is an agreement between landlord and tenant that the tenant will give up the leasehold before the term ends, terminating future obligations.

title: Surrogacy
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Surrogacy agreements are governed by statute where authorized; enforceability and legal parentage depend on compliance with statutory requirements, public policy, and judicial approval where required.

title: Survival Action
subject: Torts
ruleText: A survival action preserves the decedent's own claim and allows the estate to recover damages the decedent sustained before death.

title: Survival Actions
subject: Torts
ruleText: A survival action allows the decedent’s estate to recover damages the decedent could have recovered if alive.

title: Surviving Spouse's Share in Intestacy
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A surviving spouse's intestate share depends on the statutory scheme and whether the decedent is survived by descendants or parents, with the spouse often taking all if all surviving descendants are also descendants of the spouse and no other descendants complicate the distribution.

title: Suspect Classifications
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Race, national origin, and classifications affecting some noncitizens are suspect and generally trigger strict scrutiny.

title: Symbolic Conduct
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Expressive conduct is protected when there is an intent to convey a message likely to be understood, and government regulation unrelated to suppression of expression is valid if it furthers an important interest and is no greater than essential.

title: Symbolic Speech
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Symbolic conduct is protected as speech if the conduct is intended to convey a message and the message is likely to be understood.

title: Tacking
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Successive possessors may tack periods of possession if there is privity between them.

title: Tacking
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Successive adverse possessors may tack their periods of possession if there is privity between them, such as by voluntary transfer.

title: Takings Clause General Rule
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Private property may not be taken for public use without just compensation.

title: Taxing Power
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may tax and spend for the general welfare, and a tax is generally valid if it raises some revenue and is not a penalty in disguise prohibited by some other constitutional provision.

title: Taxing Power
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress may tax and spend for the general welfare.

title: Taxpayer Standing
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A taxpayer generally lacks standing to challenge government expenditures, except a federal taxpayer may challenge a congressional spending program alleged to violate the Establishment Clause under the narrow Flast exception.

title: Temporary Emergency Jurisdiction
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A court may exercise temporary emergency jurisdiction if the child is present in the state and the child has been abandoned or needs emergency protection because the child, a sibling, or a parent is subjected to or threatened with mistreatment or abuse.

title: Temporary Perfection — Proceeds
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A perfected security interest in proceeds remains perfected for 20 days.

title: Temporary Perfection in Proceeds
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A perfected security interest in proceeds is automatically perfected for 20 days after the security interest attaches to the proceeds. Continued perfection beyond 20 days generally requires satisfaction of one of the statutory continuation rules, such as filing covering the proceeds, same-office filing sufficiency, or identifiable cash proceeds.

title: Temporary Restraining Order
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A temporary restraining order is short term provisional relief designed to preserve the status quo until the court can consider a request for preliminary injunction.

title: Temporary Taking
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A temporary regulation that denies use of property may constitute a compensable taking.

title: Tenancy at Sufferance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy at sufferance arises when a tenant wrongfully remains in possession after the lease term ends.

title: Tenancy at Sufferance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy at sufferance arises when a tenant wrongfully remains in possession after lease termination.

title: Tenancy at Will
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy at will continues until terminated by either party without advance notice.

title: Tenancy at Will
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy at will is a leasehold of no fixed duration that continues only so long as both landlord and tenant desire.

title: Tenancy by the Entirety
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Tenancy by the entirety is a concurrent estate between married spouses with a right of survivorship and protection from unilateral transfer.

title: Tenancy by the Entirety
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Tenancy by the entirety is a marital concurrent estate with right of survivorship in which neither spouse acting alone may sever or convey the estate in most jurisdictions.

title: Tenancy for Years
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy for years lasts for a fixed, definite period of time and terminates automatically.

title: Tenancy for Years
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy for years is a leasehold for a fixed period that ends automatically at the stated date without notice.

title: Tenancy in Common
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy in common is a concurrent estate in which each cotenant owns an undivided fractional interest that is freely transferable, devisable, and descendible, and there is no right of survivorship.

title: Tenancy in Common
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenancy in common allows each co-tenant to hold an undivided interest with no right of survivorship.

title: Tenant Duties — Rent
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A tenant has a duty to pay rent as specified in the lease.

title: Tenant Remedies — Habitability
subject: Real Property
ruleText: If the implied warranty of habitability is breached, the tenant may withhold rent, repair and deduct, or terminate the lease.

title: Tenant's Duty to Pay Rent
subject: Real Property
ruleText: The tenant must pay rent as promised unless the duty is excused by a recognized landlord breach or other defense.

title: Tenth Amendment
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The Tenth Amendment reserves to the states powers not delegated to the federal government.

title: Termination by Lapse
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An offer terminates by lapse after the time stated in the offer or, if no time is stated, after a reasonable time.

title: Termination by Rejection
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A rejection by the offeree terminates the offer unless the offeror renews it.

title: Termination of Actual Authority
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Actual authority terminates by revocation by the principal, renunciation by the agent, occurrence of circumstances causing the agent reasonably to believe the principal no longer consents, completion of the task, lapse of time, or death or loss of capacity of the principal or agent.

title: Termination of Apparent Authority
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Apparent authority terminates when it is no longer reasonable for the third party to believe the agent is authorized, usually after notice from the principal or other facts that make reliance unreasonable.

title: Termination of Covenants and Servitudes
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Covenants and servitudes may terminate by release, merger, changed conditions, or abandonment.

title: Termination of Covenants and Servitudes
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Restrictive covenants and servitudes may terminate by release, merger, acquiescence, abandonment, estoppel, changed conditions, condemnation, or prescription.

title: Termination of Easement
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An easement may be terminated by release, merger, abandonment, estoppel, prescription, end of necessity, condemnation, or destruction of the servient estate.

title: Termination of Easement
subject: Real Property
ruleText: An easement may be terminated by merger, release, abandonment, prescription, or necessity ending.

title: Termination of Offer — Methods
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An offer may be terminated by revocation, rejection, counteroffer, lapse of time, or death or incapacity of either party.

title: Termination of Parental Rights
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Parental rights may be terminated only upon proof of statutory grounds such as abuse, neglect, abandonment, prolonged failure to support, or unfitness, along with compliance with due process and, typically, a best-interests determination.

title: Termination of Parental Rights
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Parental rights may be terminated upon clear and convincing evidence of abuse, neglect, abandonment, or unfitness.

title: Termination of Spousal Support
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Periodic spousal support often terminates upon the death of either party or the recipient's remarriage, and sometimes on cohabitation, depending on the statute or order.

title: Terry Stop and Frisk
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may briefly stop a person based on reasonable suspicion of criminal activity and frisk for weapons based on reasonable suspicion the person is armed and dangerous.

title: Testamentary Capacity
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A testator has testamentary capacity if, at the time the will is executed, the testator is of the required age and understands the nature of making a will, the general nature and extent of the property, the natural objects of bounty, and the disposition being made.

title: Testamentary Capacity
subject: Trusts
ruleText: Testamentary capacity requires that the testator understand the nature of the act, the extent of the property, the natural objects of bounty, and the disposition being made.

title: Testimonial Statements
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Testimonial statements include statements made during police interrogation or with the primary purpose of establishing evidence for prosecution.

title: Then-Existing Condition — Exclusion of Memory or Belief
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Statements of memory or belief are not admissible to prove the fact remembered or believed.

title: Then-Existing Mental, Emotional, or Physical Condition
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement of the declarant’s then-existing state of mind, emotion, sensation, or physical condition is admissible to prove that condition.

title: Then-Existing State of Mind
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement of the declarant's then-existing state of mind, emotion, sensation, or physical condition, such as motive, intent, plan, mental feeling, pain, or bodily health, is admissible. But it does not include a statement of memory or belief to prove the fact remembered or believed unless it relates to the validity or terms of the declarant's will.

title: Third Party Beneficiary
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A third party may enforce a contract when the contract was intended to benefit that third party and the third party's rights have vested.

title: Third Party Standing
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A litigant generally may not raise the rights of third parties, but may do so if there is a close relationship and a hindrance to the third party's ability to protect their own interests.

title: Third-Party Beneficiaries — Definition
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A third-party beneficiary is a person whom the parties to a contract intend to benefit.

title: Third-Party Custody
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Because a legal parent has a fundamental right to custody, a third party usually may obtain custody only by rebutting the parental preference through proof such as unfitness, extraordinary circumstances, or serious detriment to the child under applicable law.

title: Third-Party Visitation and Troxel
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Under Troxel, a fit parent's decision regarding third-party visitation is entitled to special weight, and the state may not override that decision without adequate justification consistent with due process.

title: Time for Marketable Title
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Absent contrary agreement, the seller need only provide marketable title at closing, not at the time the contract is signed.

title: Time for Removal
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A notice of removal generally must be filed within 30 days after the defendant receives the initial pleading or is served, depending on the procedural posture.

title: Time Is of the Essence
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Time is not of the essence in land contracts unless expressly stated.

title: Time, Place, and Manner Restrictions
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Time, place, and manner restrictions must be content-neutral, narrowly tailored, serve an important government interest, and leave open alternative channels of communication.

title: Timing — JMOL
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A motion for judgment as a matter of law must be made before the case is submitted to the jury.

title: Timing of Hearing
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A hearing may be held after deprivation if prompt post-deprivation review is provided and pre-deprivation process is impracticable.

title: Timing of Summary Judgment
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A party may move for summary judgment after 20 days from commencement of the action or after service of a motion for summary judgment.

title: Title Insurance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Title insurance indemnifies the insured against covered title defects existing at the time the policy is issued, subject to exclusions and exceptions.

title: Title Theory and Lien Theory
subject: Real Property
ruleText: In title theory states, the mortgage passes legal title to the mortgagee until satisfaction of the debt, while in lien theory states the mortgage creates only a lien and the mortgagor retains title.

title: Total Regulatory Taking
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A regulation that deprives land of all economically viable use is a taking unless the restricted use was already prohibited by background principles of state property or nuisance law.

title: Trade Name
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A financing statement that provides only the debtor's trade name does not sufficiently provide the debtor's name.

title: Traditional Bases of Personal Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Traditional bases of personal jurisdiction include service on the defendant while present in the forum, domicile in the forum, consent, and in some cases waiver.

title: Traditional Bases of Personal Jurisdiction
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Personal jurisdiction exists where the defendant is domiciled in the forum, personally served in the forum, or consents to jurisdiction.

title: Traditional Public Forums
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Streets, sidewalks, and parks are traditional public forums where speech restrictions receive the greatest protection.

title: Transactions Covered by Article 9
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Article 9 generally governs any transaction, regardless of form, that creates a security interest in personal property or fixtures by contract, as well as agricultural liens, sales of accounts, chattel paper, payment intangibles, and promissory notes, and certain consignments.

title: Transactions Excluded from Article 9
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Article 9 generally does not apply to most landlord's liens, statutory liens other than agricultural liens, assignments of wages, transfers of interests in real property except fixtures and certain related collateral, tort claims other than commercial tort claims, and many transfers by operation of law.

title: Transfer — For Convenience (1404)
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A court may transfer a case to another proper venue for the convenience of parties and witnesses and in the interest of justice.

title: Transfer — Improper Venue (1406)
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: When venue is improper, the transferee court applies its own choice-of-law rules.

title: Transfer — Transferee Court Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: After transfer for convenience, the transferee court applies the law of the transferor court.

title: Transfer for Convenience
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A federal court may transfer a case to another district where the action might have been brought if transfer serves the convenience of parties and witnesses and the interests of justice.

title: Transfer of LLC Interests
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Absent agreement otherwise, a member may transfer the member's transferable interest, which generally consists of economic rights to distributions, but the transferee does not become a member or obtain management rights without the consent required by statute or the operating agreement.

title: Transfer of Mortgaged Property
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A mortgagor may transfer property subject to the mortgage, but the mortgage remains attached.

title: Transfer of Partnership Interest
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A partner may transfer the partner's transferable interest, which generally consists of the partner's share of profits, losses, and distributions, but a transferee does not become a partner or acquire management rights without consent of the other partners.

title: Transfer of the Note
subject: Real Property
ruleText: The mortgage generally follows the note, so transfer of the debt ordinarily transfers the security interest as well.

title: Transfer or Dismiss When Venue Is Wrong
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: When venue is improper, the court may dismiss or transfer the case to a proper district if justice so requires.

title: Transfer to New Debtor in Another State
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: If collateral is transferred to a new debtor located in another jurisdiction and the security interest becomes perfected against the new debtor under the law of that jurisdiction, the security interest generally remains perfected for one year after the transfer or until perfection would otherwise lapse, whichever occurs earlier, unless reperfected sooner under the new jurisdiction's law.

title: Transferee of Collateral
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: A security interest generally follows the collateral into the hands of a transferee unless a rule cuts it off, such as buyer-in-ordinary-course protection, authorized disposition, consumer-buyer protection, or another statutory exception.

title: Transferred Cases — Choice of Law
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: After transfer for convenience, the transferee court applies the choice-of-law rules of the transferor court.

title: Transferred Cases — Improper Venue
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: After transfer due to improper venue, the transferee court applies its own choice-of-law rules.

title: Transferred Intent
subject: Torts
ruleText: Transferred intent applies when a defendant intends to commit an intentional tort against one person but causes a different intentional tort or harms a different person.

title: Transferred Intent
subject: Torts
ruleText: Transferred intent applies when the defendant intends to commit battery, assault, false imprisonment, trespass to land, or trespass to chattels against one person or property but instead commits one of those torts against another.

title: Transferred Intent
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Transferred intent applies when a defendant intends to harm one victim but accidentally harms another.

title: Transmutation
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Transmutation occurs when separate property is converted into marital or community property, or vice versa, by agreement, titling, gift, or conduct showing intent to change the property's character.

title: Treasury Shares
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: Treasury shares are shares previously issued and later reacquired by the corporation. They are generally treated as authorized but unissued shares unless the articles provide otherwise.

title: Treaty Power
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: The President negotiates treaties, which become effective with approval by two-thirds of the Senate; valid treaties are supreme law, subject to the Constitution.

title: Trespass to Airspace and Subsurface
subject: Torts
ruleText: A trespass may occur by intrusion into the immediate reaches of the landowner's airspace or into the subsurface if it interferes with possession.

title: Trespass to Chattels
subject: Torts
ruleText: Trespass to chattels occurs when a defendant intentionally interferes with another’s personal property causing dispossession or actual harm.

title: Trespass to Chattels
subject: Torts
ruleText: Trespass to chattels requires an intentional interference with the plaintiff's right of possession in personal property that causes dispossession, impairment of condition, deprivation of use, or bodily harm to the possessor or something in which the possessor has a legally protected interest.

title: Trespass to Land
subject: Torts
ruleText: Trespass to land occurs when a defendant intentionally enters or causes a physical invasion of land possessed by another.

title: Trespass to Land
subject: Torts
ruleText: Trespass to land requires an intentional physical invasion of the plaintiff's real property.

title: Trespassing Animals
subject: Torts
ruleText: The owner of livestock is strictly liable for damage caused by trespassing animals.

title: Trial — Right to Jury Trial
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: The Seventh Amendment preserves the right to a jury trial in civil actions at law.

title: True Threats
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A true threat is a serious expression of intent to commit unlawful violence against a person or group, and such threats are unprotected by the First Amendment.

title: Trust — General Rule
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A trust is a fiduciary relationship in which a trustee holds legal title for the benefit of beneficiaries.

title: Trust Creation Requirements
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A valid trust requires intent, identifiable trust property, a trustee, and identifiable beneficiaries.

title: Trust Modification — Cy Pres
subject: Trusts
ruleText: Cy pres allows modification of a charitable trust to approximate the settlor’s intent.

title: Trust Property
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A trust must have trust property, also called the res, that is presently identified and transferable to the trustee or held by the settlor as trustee.

title: Trust Termination
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A trust may terminate when its purpose is fulfilled or becomes impossible.

title: Trust Validity and Administration
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Trust validity and administration may be governed by the law designated in the trust instrument or, absent a valid designation, by the law of the jurisdiction with the most significant relationship to the trust or its administration.

title: Trustee Duties — Care
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust with reasonable care and skill.

title: Trustee Duties — Impartiality
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A trustee must act impartially among beneficiaries.

title: Trustee Duties — Loyalty
subject: Trusts
ruleText: A trustee owes a duty of loyalty and must act solely in the beneficiaries’ interests.

title: Trustee Duty of Loyalty
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust solely in the interests of the beneficiaries and avoid self-dealing, conflicts of interest, and transactions affected by personal interests unless properly authorized or consented to.
buzzwords: solely in the interests of beneficiaries

title: Trustee Duty of Prudence
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A trustee must administer the trust with the care, skill, and caution that a prudent person would use in similar circumstances, taking into account the purposes, terms, and beneficiaries of the trust.

title: Truth as Defense
subject: Torts
ruleText: Truth is an absolute defense to defamation.

title: Types of Disabilities
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Recognized disabilities include minority, mental incapacity, and imprisonment.

title: Types of Notice
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Notice may be actual notice, inquiry notice arising from facts that would prompt a reasonable investigation, or record notice arising from properly recorded instruments in the chain of title.

title: Types of Notice — Actual Notice
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Actual notice exists when a purchaser has direct knowledge of a prior interest.

title: Types of Notice — Inquiry Notice
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Inquiry notice exists when circumstances would prompt a reasonable purchaser to investigate.

title: Types of Notice — Record Notice
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Record notice exists when a prior interest is properly recorded.

title: Types of Principals
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A principal may be disclosed, partially disclosed, or undisclosed. A disclosed principal is known to the third party and the third party knows the agent acts for a principal. A partially disclosed principal is known to exist but not identified. An undisclosed principal is not known to exist.

title: Types of Spousal Support
subject: Family Law
ruleText: Common forms of spousal support include temporary, rehabilitative, reimbursement, permanent or long-term periodic, and lump-sum support, depending on statutory standards and the parties' circumstances.

title: UCC Contract Modification
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, consideration is not required to modify a contract for the sale of goods if the modification is made in good faith.

title: UCC Parol Evidence Rule
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, a final written agreement may be explained or supplemented by course of performance, course of dealing, or usage of trade unless carefully negated.

title: UCC Remedies — Buyer
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, a buyer may cover, recover damages, or seek specific performance upon seller’s breach.

title: UCC Remedies — Seller
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, a seller may withhold delivery, resell goods, recover damages, or cancel the contract upon buyer’s breach.

title: UCC Sale of Goods for $500 or More
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract for the sale of goods for $500 or more is generally unenforceable unless there is a sufficient signed writing indicating a contract for sale.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Admission Exception
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds is satisfied to the extent that a party admits in court that a contract for sale was made.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — General Rule
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, a contract for the sale of goods priced at $500 or more must be evidenced by a signed writing to be enforceable.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Merchant Confirmation
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Between merchants, a written confirmation sent within a reasonable time satisfies the Statute of Frauds unless written notice of objection is given within ten days.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Part Performance
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, part performance satisfies the Statute of Frauds to the extent that goods have been paid for or accepted.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Specially Manufactured Goods
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The Statute of Frauds does not apply when goods are specially manufactured for the buyer and are not suitable for sale to others.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds — Writing Requirements
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Under the UCC, the writing must indicate a contract for sale and state a quantity term.

title: UCC Statute of Frauds Exceptions
subject: Contracts
ruleText: UCC exceptions include specially manufactured goods, admissions in pleadings or testimony, and goods paid for or accepted.

title: UCCJEA and Custody Jurisdiction
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Interstate child-custody jurisdiction is generally governed by the UCCJEA, which prioritizes the child's home state and limits modification by other states once a proper state has continuing exclusive jurisdiction.

title: UIFSA and Child Support
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Interstate child-support enforcement and modification are generally governed by UIFSA, which seeks to ensure one controlling support order and continuing exclusive jurisdiction in the proper issuing state.

title: UIFSA and Interstate Support
subject: Family Law
ruleText: UIFSA governs establishment, enforcement, and modification of child-support orders across state lines by providing rules for issuing, registering, enforcing, and determining controlling support orders.

title: Ultimate Issue
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Opinion testimony is not objectionable just because it embraces an ultimate issue, but in a criminal case an expert may not state an opinion about whether the defendant did or did not have a mental state or condition that constitutes an element of the crime or defense.

title: Ultimate Issue
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Expert testimony is not objectionable merely because it embraces an ultimate issue to be decided by the trier of fact.

title: Unanimity
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A criminal jury verdict must be unanimous.

title: Unanimity Rule for Removal
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: All properly joined and served defendants generally must join in or consent to removal.
buzzwords: join in or consent to removal

title: Unavailability
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A declarant is unavailable if the declarant is exempted from testifying due to privilege, refuses despite a court order, testifies to not remembering the subject matter, cannot be present due to death or then-existing physical or mental illness or infirmity, or is absent and the statement's proponent has not been able, by process or other reasonable means, to procure the declarant's attendance or testimony.

title: Unconscionability
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract or term is unenforceable if it is unconscionable due to unfair surprise and oppressive terms.

title: Unconscionability
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A court may refuse to enforce a contract or clause that was unconscionable at the time it was made, often involving unfair surprise and oppressive terms.

title: Undue Influence
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Undue influence occurs when a party uses excessive pressure to overcome the free will of another who is in a confidential relationship.

title: Undue Influence
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: A will or provision is invalid for undue influence if the influencer exerted such pressure that it overcame the testator's free will and caused a disposition the testator otherwise would not have made.

title: Undue Influence
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract is voidable for undue influence when unfair persuasion by a dominant party overcomes the will of a vulnerable party.

title: Undue Influence
subject: Trusts
ruleText: Undue influence exists when influence overcomes the testator’s free will.

title: Unforeseeable Misuse
subject: Torts
ruleText: A defendant is not liable for injuries caused by an unforeseeable misuse of a product.

title: Unilateral Contract
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A unilateral contract is accepted by complete performance of the requested act.

title: Unilateral Mistake
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Unilateral mistake may justify avoidance if the mistake concerns a basic assumption, has a material effect, the mistaken party did not bear the risk, and enforcement would be unconscionable or the other party knew or caused the mistake.

title: Unilateral Mistake
subject: Contracts
ruleText: A contract is voidable for unilateral mistake when one party is mistaken and the other party knew or should have known of the mistake.

title: Unmarketable Title — Defects
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Title is unmarketable if it is subject to encumbrances, adverse possession claims, or zoning violations.

title: Unperfected Versus Unperfected
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: As between conflicting unperfected security interests, priority generally goes to the first to attach or become enforceable.

title: Unprotected Speech — Categories
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Unprotected speech includes incitement, obscenity, fighting words, defamation, and true threats.

title: Unreasonableness — Balancing Test
subject: Torts
ruleText: Unreasonableness in nuisance is determined by balancing the gravity of the harm against the utility of the defendant’s conduct.

title: Usage of Trade
subject: Contracts
ruleText: Usage of trade refers to practices or methods of dealing regularly observed in a particular trade or industry.

title: Use of Illegally Obtained Evidence — Impeachment
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Illegally obtained evidence may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony but not to impeach other witnesses.

title: Use of Statements — Case-in-Chief
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of Miranda are inadmissible in the prosecution’s case-in-chief.

title: Use of Statements — Impeachment
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of Miranda may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony if voluntary.

title: Use of Statements — Sixth Amendment Violation
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of the Sixth Amendment right to counsel are inadmissible in the prosecution’s case-in-chief.

title: Use of Statements. — Impeachment
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Statements obtained in violation of the Sixth Amendment may be used to impeach the defendant’s testimony if voluntary.

title: Use of Writings on Cross
subject: Evidence
ruleText: When examining a witness about a prior statement or other writing, the examiner need not show it or disclose its contents to the witness at that time, but on request must show it or disclose its contents to opposing counsel.
buzzwords: must show opposing counsel on request

title: Usury and Contracts
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Courts often uphold a contract against a usury challenge if the contract bears a reasonable relationship to a jurisdiction whose law would validate the interest rate, unless doing so would violate a strong public policy of the forum.

title: Uttering a Forged Instrument
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Uttering occurs when a defendant knowingly uses or presents a forged instrument with intent to defraud.

title: Vagueness
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A law is void for vagueness if it fails to give reasonable notice of what is prohibited or invites arbitrary and discriminatory enforcement.

title: Vagueness Doctrine
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: A law is unconstitutionally vague if it fails to give fair notice of prohibited conduct or encourages arbitrary enforcement.

title: Valid Assignments
subject: Contracts
ruleText: An assignment is valid unless it materially alters the obligor’s duty, materially increases the burden or risk, or is prohibited by law or contract.

title: Validity of Marriage
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: A marriage that is valid where celebrated is generally valid everywhere, unless it violates a strong public policy of the forum, such as bigamy or incest under local law.

title: Validity of Premarital Agreements
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A premarital agreement is generally enforceable if entered voluntarily, after fair and reasonable disclosure or waiver of disclosure, and if the agreement is not unconscionable under applicable law.

title: Value
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Value includes any consideration sufficient to support a simple contract or an antecedent debt.

title: Value Given
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Value includes a binding commitment to extend credit, the extension of credit, acceptance of delivery under a preexisting contract, and generally any consideration sufficient to support a simple contract.

title: Variance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A variance permits land use that deviates from zoning restrictions upon a showing of undue hardship unique to the property and consistency with the public welfare, subject to jurisdictional standards.

title: Vehicle Search Incident to Arrest
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police may search a vehicle incident to arrest if the arrestee is unsecured and within reaching distance of the passenger compartment, or if it is reasonable to believe evidence of the offense of arrest might be found inside.

title: Vehicular Manslaughter
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Vehicular manslaughter occurs when a death results from negligent or reckless operation of a motor vehicle.

title: Venue
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Venue is proper in a district where any defendant resides if all defendants reside in the same state, or in a district where a substantial part of the events or omissions giving rise to the claim occurred.

title: Venue — General Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Venue is proper in a judicial district where any defendant resides if all defendants reside in the same state, where a substantial part of the events occurred, or where any defendant is subject to personal jurisdiction if no other district is available.

title: Venue — Multiple Defendants
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: When all defendants reside in the same state, venue is proper in any district where any defendant resides.

title: Vertical Privity
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Vertical privity requires a successor to hold the same estate as the original party.

title: Vested Remainder
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A vested remainder is a remainder held by an ascertained person with no condition precedent.

title: Vested Remainder
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A remainder is vested if it is given to an ascertained person and is not subject to a condition precedent other than the natural termination of the preceding estate.

title: Vested Remainder Subject to Complete Divestment
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A vested remainder subject to complete divestment is vested in an ascertained person but may be entirely divested by a subsequent condition.

title: Vested Remainder Subject to Open
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A vested remainder subject to open is a vested remainder held by at least one ascertained member of a class that may increase in size.

title: Vesting of Third-Party Rights
subject: Contracts
ruleText: The rights of an intended beneficiary vest when the beneficiary manifests assent, materially changes position in reliance, or brings suit to enforce the contract.

title: Viewpoint Discrimination
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Viewpoint discrimination occurs when the government allows speech on a subject but suppresses one side of the debate, and such discrimination is almost always unconstitutional.

title: Void or Invalid Gifts
subject: Trusts and Estates
ruleText: If a gift is void or invalid, the property usually falls into the residue, or passes by intestacy if the residue does not dispose of it.

title: Voidable Marriage
subject: Family Law
ruleText: A marriage is voidable when a defect such as fraud, duress, temporary incapacity, or nonage makes the marriage subject to challenge by a proper party, but the marriage remains valid unless and until annulled.

title: Voluntariness — Police Coercion
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Police coercion is required for a confession to be deemed involuntary.

title: Voluntariness of Confessions
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A confession is involuntary if police coercion, viewed under the totality of circumstances, overbears the defendant's will.

title: Voluntary Act
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Criminal liability requires a voluntary bodily movement or a qualifying omission where there is a legal duty to act.

title: Voluntary Intoxication
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Voluntary intoxication is a defense only if it negates specific intent; it is no defense to general intent, malice, recklessness, or strict liability crimes.

title: Voluntary Manslaughter
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Voluntary manslaughter is an intentional killing mitigated by adequate provocation or heat of passion.

title: Voluntary Manslaughter
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Murder is reduced to voluntary manslaughter when committed in the heat of passion upon adequate provocation before reasonable cooling time and before actual cooling.

title: Voluntary Waste
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Voluntary waste is affirmative conduct that reduces the property's value, such as exploitation of natural resources beyond permitted exceptions.

title: Voting Trusts
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A voting trust is an arrangement by which shareholders transfer legal title of their shares to a trustee who votes the shares pursuant to the trust agreement, while the beneficial owners retain economic interests.

title: Waiver of Attorney-Client Privilege
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The attorney-client privilege may be waived by voluntary disclosure of the privileged communication to a third party, by failure to maintain confidentiality, or by placing the communication at issue.

title: Waiver of Certain Rule 12 Defenses
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Personal jurisdiction, venue, process, and service of process defenses are generally waived if not timely asserted.

title: Waiver of Miranda Rights
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A Miranda waiver must be knowing, voluntary, and intelligent.

title: Waiver of Right to Counsel
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A defendant may waive the Sixth Amendment right to counsel if the waiver is knowing, voluntary, and intelligent.

title: Waiver of Rule 12 Defenses
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Certain Rule 12 defenses are waived if not raised in the first Rule 12 response.

title: Waiver of Service
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A defendant may waive service of process by returning a waiver request, avoiding formal service.

title: Waiver of Service
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: A defendant who waives formal service does not waive objections to venue or jurisdiction and receives additional time to respond.

title: War and Related Powers
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Congress has powers to declare war, raise and support armies, provide for the navy, call forth and regulate the militia, and make rules concerning captures and military governance.

title: Warrant — Execution
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A warrant must be executed reasonably and within the scope authorized by the warrant.

title: Warrant — General Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A valid warrant must be issued by a neutral and detached magistrate upon probable cause and must particularly describe the place to be searched and the items to be seized.

title: Warrant — Particularity Requirement
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A warrant must particularly describe the place to be searched and the persons or things to be seized.
buzzwords: persons or things to be seized

title: Warrant Exceptions — General Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A warrantless search is presumptively unreasonable unless it falls within a recognized exception to the warrant requirement.

title: Warrant Requirements
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: A warrant must be issued by a neutral and detached magistrate, supported by probable cause, and describe with particularity the place to be searched and items or persons to be seized.

title: Waste
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Waste is conduct by a holder of a possessory estate, such as a life tenant, that unreasonably harms the interests of future interest holders.

title: Wealth and Education
subject: Constitutional Law
ruleText: Wealth is generally not a suspect classification and education is not a fundamental right, so ordinary distinctions in school funding usually receive rational basis review.

title: Well-Pleaded Complaint Rule
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Federal question jurisdiction must appear on the face of the plaintiff’s well-pleaded complaint.

title: Wharton's Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Wharton's Rule bars conspiracy liability when the target crime necessarily requires concerted criminal activity by the same number of persons involved, unless more persons participate than necessary.

title: What Counts as a Statement
subject: Evidence
ruleText: A statement is a person's oral assertion, written assertion, or nonverbal conduct if the person intended it as an assertion.

title: What Is Testimonial?
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Statements are testimonial when made with the primary purpose of creating an out-of-court substitute for trial testimony, such as formal statements to police during investigation, affidavits, depositions, prior testimony, and forensic certificates prepared for prosecution. Statements made during an ongoing emergency are generally nontestimonial.

title: When a Required Party Cannot Be Joined
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: If a required party cannot be joined, the court decides whether to proceed without that party or dismiss the action after considering fairness and practical factors.

title: Where to File
subject: Secured Transactions
ruleText: Except for certain fixture filings, timber to be cut, and as-extracted collateral, a financing statement is generally filed in the central filing office of the debtor's location.

title: Wild Animals
subject: Torts
ruleText: A possessor of a wild animal is strictly liable for harm caused by the animal's dangerous propensities.

title: Wild Deed
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A wild deed is a recorded deed not connected to the chain of title, and it generally does not give record notice to later purchasers.

title: Withdrawal by Accomplice
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: An accomplice withdraws by repudiating prior aid, attempting to neutralize assistance, and timely communicating withdrawal before the crime is committed.

title: Withdrawal From Conspiracy
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: Withdrawal requires affirmative notice to co-conspirators or law enforcement in time to allow abandonment, but it usually does not erase liability for the conspiracy already completed.

title: Work Product — Opinion Work Product
subject: Evidence
ruleText: Opinion work product reflecting an attorney’s mental impressions or legal theories receives heightened protection.

title: Work Product Doctrine
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Materials prepared in anticipation of litigation are protected from discovery unless substantial need and undue hardship are shown.

title: Work Product Doctrine
subject: Evidence
ruleText: The work product doctrine protects materials prepared in anticipation of litigation from disclosure.

title: Work Product Doctrine
subject: Civil Procedure
ruleText: Work product protects materials prepared in anticipation of litigation from discovery unless the requesting party shows substantial need and undue hardship in obtaining the substantial equivalent by other means.

title: Wrongful Death
subject: Torts
ruleText: A wrongful death action is a statutory claim brought by designated survivors for their own losses resulting from the decedent's death caused by the defendant's tort.

title: Wrongful Death
subject: Torts
ruleText: Wrongful death allows recovery by statutory beneficiaries for damages resulting from a decedent’s death caused by negligence.

title: Wrongful Death and Damages
subject: Conflict of Laws
ruleText: Wrongful death and damages issues are analyzed under the forum's choice-of-law methodology, and some courts may distinguish between liability rules and damages limitations when selecting the governing law.

title: Wrongful Dissociation
subject: Business Associations
ruleText: A partner's dissociation is wrongful if it breaches an express term of the partnership agreement or occurs before expiration of a term or completion of an undertaking in certain ways, such as wrongful withdrawal or bankruptcy where treated as wrongful by statute or agreement.

title: Year-and-a-Day Rule
subject: Criminal Law and Procedure
ruleText: At common law, homicide liability required the victim to die within a year and a day of the defendant's act, though most jurisdictions have abolished the rule.

title: Zoning — Constitutionality
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Zoning ordinances are constitutional if they are rationally related to a legitimate government purpose.

title: Zoning — General Rule
subject: Real Property
ruleText: Zoning ordinances regulate land use pursuant to the state’s police power.

title: Zoning — Use Variance vs Area Variance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A use variance permits a prohibited land use, while an area variance allows deviation from dimensional requirements.

title: Zoning — Variance
subject: Real Property
ruleText: A variance permits deviation from a zoning ordinance when strict enforcement would cause undue hardship.
